Text: You have Enlisted into the Brotherhood of Steel.

Initiate, this is a message from your chapter elder. I would like to congratulate you on passing your trials. You have been picked to be a part of the Brotherhood, do not make the same mistake as our forbearers.

 You will be issued a standard salary every week for you and your team as well as provisions that can be picked up at our bases when you are not on patrol or finding lost technology. The {FACTION_name} has bunkers and facilities in order to better train your team, use them wisely.

 -Ad Victoriam

This is a techprint for the research project '{PROJECT_label}'.

The physical form of techprints can range from scraps of paper with diagrams, all the way to advanced self-contained teaching machines. Regardless of form, they play the same role. They give your researchers the confidence that an idea is possible, and a rough idea of the designs used in more established societies.

Your researchers need the foundational knowledge offered by techprints to know where to start on a given technology. Only after enough techprints have been applied can your researchers work out the fine details of how to make use of the technology in your particular situation.

Applying techprints to a satisfied project fulfills half the remaining research progress. In addition, researchers always gain a boost to their intellectual skills when applying techprints, even on already-completed technologies.

After many years of devoted service in the Brotherhood, [PAWN_nameDef] became part of the inner circle of [PAWN_possessive] chapter. [PAWN_pronoun] quickly became a trusted advisor for the Elder, whose opinion was highly sought after. Many of [PAWN_possessive] brothers and sisters believed, that [PAWN_pronoun] would one day become the new Elder.

Raised within the fortified halls of a Brotherhood bunker, [PAWN_nameDef] was expected to follow [PAWN_possessive] parents into the Scribes, dedicating a life to maintaining technology. But [PAWN_pronoun] longed for the battlefield, inspired by stories of Paladins reclaiming lost knowledge from the wasteland. Defying expectations, [PAWN_pronoun] fought to join the initiates, proving [PAWN_possessive] worth through relentless training and a hunger for fieldwork. While [PAWN_possessive] technical knowledge remains sharp, [PAWN_pronoun] now seeks to earn a place among the Brotherhoodâ€™s elite warriors.

Born in the unforgiving wasteland, [PAWN_nameDef] grew up scavenging ruins and avoiding raider gangs. When a Brotherhood patrol found [PAWN_pronoun] fending off a group of feral ghouls with nothing but a rusted pistol, they saw potential. Given the choice between enlistment or exile, [PAWN_pronoun] embraced the Brotherhoodâ€™s ideals, eager to trade a life of survival for one of purpose. Now, as an aspirant, [PAWN_pronoun] trains to uphold the Brotherhoodâ€™s doctrine and earn the right to wear power armor.

From an early age, [PAWN_nameDef] was immersed in the Brotherhoodâ€™s teachings not just in battle, but in the knowledge that technology must be preserved at all costs. Unlike the wastelanders who fumbled with old world machines, [PAWN_possessive] lessons came from preserved databanks and the Brotherhoodâ€™s most skilled Scribes. Though [PAWN_pronoun] has trained in combat like all Brotherhood recruits, [PAWN_possessive] true passion lies in deciphering ancient schematics and uncovering lost technology. But in a world where knowledge alone wonâ€™t keep [PAWN_pronoun] alive, [PAWN_pronoun] is determined to prove [PAWN_possessive] worth as a warrior and a scholar.

Unlike most aspirants, [PAWN_nameDef] wasnâ€™t drawn to war, but to the machines that powered it. From childhood, [PAWN_possessive] days were spent studying fusion cores, repairing damaged laser rifles, and testing salvaged pre-war technology. While the Brotherhood needed warriors, it also needed minds capable of keeping their advanced arsenal operational. Though [PAWN_pronoun] trained in combat like all aspirants, [PAWN_pronoun] finds just as much satisfaction in the hum of a properly calibrated laser as [PAWN_pronoun] does in the heat of battle.

Since the day [PAWN_pronoun] could walk, [PAWN_nameDef] wanted to become a Paladin and earn the privilege of wearing one of the Brotherhoodâ€™s finest power-armour. However, the road to this goal proved to be long and difficult and so [PAWN_nameDef] trained even harder than the other squires.

[PAWN_nameDef] was always in charge of [PAWN_possessive] clique. [PAWN_possessive] peers looked up to [PAWN_objective] not because of sheer brawn, but because of [PAWN_possessive] wisdom and natural charisma. Many knights and paladins saw these as the qualities of a future elder.

[PAWN_nameDef]â€™s parents joined the Brotherhood after [PAWN_pronoun] was born, bringing their child into the group. [PAWN_nameDef] tried [PAWN_possessive] best to get along with the other squires, but never managed to be wholly accepted as one of their own.

The wasteland was full of Super Mutants, Ghouls and other abominations the Brotherhood sought to purge. [PAWN_nameDef] was part of a team deployed to cleanse the wasteland of the radiated horrors. [PAWN_pronoun] was an expert fighter, never losing [PAWN_possessive] nerve in battle. After years of fighting the vilest creatures the wasteland spat out, [PAWN_nameDef] developed an almost religious zealotry which separated [PAWN_objective] from the more pragmatic members of the Brotherhood.

The Brotherhood of Steel had a long and proud history and [PAWN_nameDef] was honoured to teach the squires about it. While [PAWN_pronoun] was a strict teacher, [PAWN_pronoun] was still beloved by [PAWN_possessive] pupils and respected by the adult members of [PAWN_possessive] chapter for [PAWN_possessive] extensive knowledge of the past.

The strict military lifestyle of the Brotherhood was not for everyone. [PAWN_nameDef] had trouble adapting to the stringent, regulated routine within the organisation. [PAWN_pronoun] longed for freedom, creative activities, and self-actualization.

[PAWN_nameDef] was member of the BOS, who adhered to the preservationist ideology. The preservationists were often seen as followers of a more idealistic vision for the Brotherhood of Steel. While they still thought that theyâ€™re main goal was to reclaim lost technology, they often tried to help people outside of the Brotherhood too, since they thought of themselves as guardians and protectors of the whole of humanity.

The Brotherhood wouldnâ€™t be the Brotherhood without their heavy suits of power armour and laser weapons. [PAWN_nameDef] was a quartermaster for [PAWN_possessive] chapter and fulfilled this duty with great pride. While all soldiers of the BOS were expected to look after their own gear, [PAWN_nameDef] was the person they went to as soon as their equipmentâ€™s damages exceeded their own repairing capabilities.

[PAWN_nameDef] was member of the BOS, who adhered to the reactionary ideology. The reactionaries were the most inward oriented group within the Brotherhood. They usually didnâ€™t care much about the common wastelanders, as long as they didnâ€™t get in their way. In their mind, the Brotherhood was the only group who should be allowed to possess pre-war military technology. They didnâ€™t shy away from going to war with other groups, if they refused to give up what the Brotherhood desired.

The main purpose of the Brotherhood of Steel was the reclaiming of lost technologies from the old world. [PAWN_nameDef] was part of a team of Brotherhood soldiers tasked with reclaiming caches of technology from pre-war buildings. It was an extremely dangerous task, since most structurally intact ruins were infested by ghouls, mutants, raiders and other vermin.

[PAWN_nameDef] was member of the BOS, who adhered to the restorer ideology. The restorers were a rather young ideology within the Brotherhood. They tried to combine elements of both the preservationist and reactionary ideology. Their most distinctive feature was their approach to the politics of the wasteland since they tried to claim dominion over the people of the wasteland. Since they thought of themselves as the only hope for the continued survival of humankind, their goal was to establish a strong presence within their domain to ensure that the common people of the wasteland obeyed their laws.

[PAWN_nameDef] was a scribe with the Brotherhood of Steel. [PAWN_pronoun] received advanced education in many fields such as medicine, engineering, biology and the history of the Brotherhood. [PAWN_pronoun] wasnâ€™t much of a fighter, which wasnâ€™t necessary since combat duties were carried out by the military branch of [PAWN_possessive] chapter.

[PAWN_nameDef] was a squire within the Brotherhood of Steel. [PAWN_pronoun] was trained and educated by senior members. While the Brotherhood puts an emphasis on military training, all squires receive some formal education, a privilege many inhabitants of the Wastelands did not enjoy.

While the Brotherhood usually shunned luxuries, the elders did acknowledge the boost of morale a fine, or at least not revolting, meal offered. As a squire, [PAWN_nameDef] showed a talent for cooking, especially in the large portions the Brotherhoodâ€™s canteens needed to feed its members and regularly helped out the staff in [PAWN_possessive] spare time.

After decades of service, [PAWN_nameDef] rose through the Brotherhood's ranks to become an Elder. [PAWN_pronoun] has witnessed the rise and fall of many chapters, the loss of countless brothers and sisters, and the endless struggle to preserve humanity's technological heritage. The weight of leadership has changed [PAWN_objective] - where once [PAWN_pronoun] was a fierce warrior, now [PAWN_pronoun] is a careful strategist who must balance the Brotherhood's mission with the lives under [PAWN_possessive] command.

[PAWN_nameDef] became an Elder not through political maneuvering, but through sheer competence and unwavering dedication to the Brotherhood's ideals. [PAWN_pronoun] has led countless expeditions into the wasteland, secured vital technology caches, and negotiated with both allies and enemies. Though [PAWN_possessive] body shows the wear of decades in power armor, [PAWN_possessive] mind remains sharp and [PAWN_possessive] resolve unshakeable. To [PAWN_objective], the Brotherhood is not just an organizationâ€”it is humanity's last hope.

Power corrupts, and [PAWN_nameDef] has felt its influence. What began as righteous leadership of a Brotherhood chapter slowly transformed into authoritarian control. [PAWN_pronoun] justifies every harsh decision, every sacrifice of subordinates, as necessary for the greater good. The Brotherhood's mission has become [PAWN_possessive] personal crusade, and [PAWN_pronoun] will crush anyoneâ€”within or without the organizationâ€”who stands in [PAWN_possessive] way. Some whisper that [PAWN_pronoun] has forgotten what they're fighting to preserve.

[PAWN_nameDef] showed signs of above average intelligence while being educated by the Brotherhoodâ€™s Scribes. [PAWN_pronoun] was soon recruited into their ranks where [PAWN_pronoun] could put [PAWN_possessive] mental capabilities to good use. [PAWN_pronoun] may not have been the best fighter the Brotherhood had to offer, but that was the knightâ€™s task anyway.

From the moment [PAWN_nameDef] was deemed worthy of Knight status, [PAWN_pronoun] dedicated [PAWN_possessive] life to perfecting battlefield tactics. While others sought glory, [PAWN_pronoun] understood that the Brotherhoodâ€™s true strength was in strategy and discipline. Every battle was another test, every engagement another lesson. Though [PAWN_pronoun] holds respect for Brotherhood ideals, [PAWN_pronoun] values cold efficiency over blind loyalty. In the end, only the strongest deserve survival.

Unlike many of [PAWN_possessive] peers, [PAWN_nameDef] never saw battle as duty it was a privilege. From [PAWN_possessive] first kill as an Initiate, [PAWN_pronoun] learned to revel in combat, the thrill of watching enemies fall beneath superior firepower. While others spoke of honor, [PAWN_pronoun] focused on efficiency. If the Brotherhood demanded violence, then [PAWN_pronoun] would be the first to deliver it, without hesitation or remorse. Some whisper that [PAWN_pronoun] enjoys war too much but the Brotherhood needs killers just as much as it needs strategists.

[PAWN_nameDef] has lived by one philosophy: weakness is death. Raised by a Paladin who survived countless campaigns, [PAWN_pronoun] was taught that mercy was just another form of hesitation. By the time [PAWN_pronoun] became a Knight, [PAWN_pronoun] had already survived more firefights than many ever would. Now, clad in Brotherhood armor, [PAWN_pronoun] fights without fear, believing that only war can shape a soldier into something greater.

There was a time when [PAWN_nameDef] believed in the Brotherhoodâ€™s mission without question. Technology was sacred, and its protection was a noble duty. But after years on the battlefield, after watching good soldiers die over scraps of machinery, doubt crept in. Was the Brotherhood truly saving humanity, or just hoarding power? Though [PAWN_pronoun] still fights, a deep cynicism has set in. The mission continues, but faith is a distant memory.

To [PAWN_nameDef], the wasteland is a plague, and it must be cleansed. Mutants, raiders, ghouls none of them deserve to exist. [PAWN_pronoun] believes the Brotherhoodâ€™s true mission is extermination, a purge to restore what humanity once was. Where others hesitate, [PAWN_pronoun] strikes without mercy, convinced that every kill brings the world one step closer to order.
[PAWN_nameDef] specialized in maintaining the Citadel's defenses and infrastructure, ensuring the Pride's stronghold remained secure against all threats.
[PAWN_nameDef] kept the Citadel fed, preparing hearty meals for Knights and Scribes and boosting morale with every meal served for the Pride.
[PAWN_nameDef] specialized in explosives, clearing rubble and disabling enemy fortifications for the Pride's operations in the Capital Wasteland.
[PAWN_nameDef] trained new recruits in weapons handling, tactics, and survival, shaping the next generation of Lyons' Pride warriors.
[PAWN_nameDef] maintained the Pride's vehicles and power armor, ensuring every machine was ready for battle at a moment's notice.
[PAWN_nameDef] mastered the use and maintenance of power armor, leading charges against super mutants and protecting Lyons' Pride interests with unmatched resilience.
[PAWN_nameDef] managed the Pride's armory, distributing weapons and supplies to Knights and Scribes, and ensuring nothing was wasted in the fight for survival.
[PAWN_nameDef] excelled at scouting enemy positions and gathering intelligence, often venturing deep into hostile territory to ensure the Pride's tactical advantage.
[PAWN_nameDef] led expeditions into dangerous ruins, recovering valuable technology and resources for the Pride's continued survival.
[PAWN_nameDef] led reconnaissance teams through the ruins of DC, mapping safe routes and identifying threats before they could strike for the Pride.

After years of training, [PAWN_nameDef] became a full-fledged Knight, patrolling the ruins of DC and defending settlements from raiders and mutants. [PAWN_pronoun] earned a reputation for bravery and tactical skill among the Lyons' Pride.

[PAWN_nameDef] fought alongside the legendary Lyons' Pride, facing the Capital Wasteland's greatest threats. [PAWN_pronoun] is respected for [PAWN_possessive] courage and unwavering loyalty to the Pride's ideals.
[PAWN_nameDef] was tasked with tracking and confronting Outcasts, enforcing Elder Lyons' vision and protecting the unity of the Pride.
[PAWN_nameDef] dedicated [PAWN_possessive] life to preserving the Pride's history and cataloging every piece of recovered technology and lore.
[PAWN_nameDef] maintained radio contact between patrols and the Citadel, coordinating missions and relaying vital information for the Pride across the wasteland.
[PAWN_nameDef] negotiated with wasteland settlements and rival factions on behalf of Lyons' Pride, forging alliances and securing resources for the cause.
[PAWN_nameDef] traveled the wasteland to study mutated flora and fauna, providing the Pride with valuable scientific insights and survival knowledge.
[PAWN_nameDef] focused on developing new treatments and medicines, using both pre-war knowledge and wasteland discoveries to heal the Pride's wounded.
Dedicated to saving lives, [PAWN_nameDef] became a medical specialist, treating wounded Lyons' Pride members and researching advanced healing techniques.
As a Scribe, [PAWN_nameDef] scoured the wasteland for lost knowledge and pre-war technology, documenting every discovery for the Lyons' Pride archives.
[PAWN_nameDef] spent [PAWN_possessive] early years helping Pride archivists preserve and study pre-war documents, learning the importance of history and record-keeping.

[PAWN_nameDef] was born and raised within the fortified walls of the Citadel. From a young age, [PAWN_pronoun] learned the value of discipline, order, and the importance of technology in rebuilding civilization as part of Lyons' Pride.
While the Pride shunned luxuries, the elders acknowledged the morale boost a decent meal offered. As a squire, [PAWN_nameDef] showed a talent for cooking and regularly helped out the canteen staff.
Assigned to the medbay, [PAWN_nameDef] learned basic first aid and battlefield triage, preparing [PAWN_objective] for the harsh realities of wasteland combat as part of Lyons' Pride.
[PAWN_nameDef] assisted Pride medics with basic first aid and learned to care for the wounded, developing a steady hand and a compassionate heart.
[PAWN_nameDef] learned responsibility and teamwork by helping organize meals and supplies in the Pride's mess hall, ensuring everyone was fed and accounted for.
[PAWN_nameDef] was trusted to deliver messages and supplies throughout the Citadel, learning the importance of reliability and quick thinking in Pride operations.
From a young age, [PAWN_nameDef] was fascinated by the Pride's iconic power armor. [PAWN_pronoun] spent countless hours learning to maintain and eventually operate these technological marvels.
From a young age, [PAWN_nameDef] was taught to navigate the ruins of DC, learning stealth, observation, and survival skills from Pride scouts.
[PAWN_nameDef] was chosen by a Pride Scribe for early mentorship, learning to read, write, and analyze ancient texts and schematics.
Fascinated by pre-war relics, [PAWN_nameDef] spent [PAWN_possessive] youth assisting Pride Scribes in restoring ancient technology, learning the value of knowledge and patience.
Orphaned by raiders, [PAWN_nameDef] was rescued by a Lyons' Pride patrol and given a new purpose. Life in the Citadel was strict, but [PAWN_pronoun] thrived under the Pride's guidance.
As a child, [PAWN_nameDef] was often found in the Pride's workshops, learning to repair weapons and armor under the watchful eyes of veteran mechanics.
[PAWN_nameDef] was always curious about how things worked, often found disassembling and reassembling gadgets in the Pride's workshops under the guidance of patient Knights.

[PAWN_nameDef] always had an interest in the history of mankind, much to the irritation of [PAWN_possessive] teachers, as the Pride preferred to teach more â€œusefulâ€ things. [PAWN_pronoun] often procured knowledge through unconventional means.
Maintained and repaired the chapter's weapons and armor during the lean years in Hidden Valley. Became an expert at keeping aging equipment functional with minimal resources and salvaged components.

Cataloged pre-war technology and maintained the bunker's systems during the years of isolation. Watched helplessly as equipment failures mounted and spare parts dwindled. Became an expert at jury-rigging solutions with whatever materials were available.
Spent childhood studying in the Brotherhood's archives, learning about pre-war technology and the chapter's history. Memorized technical manuals and the Codex while dreaming of the surface world.

Born and raised in the confines of Hidden Valley bunker during the chapter's isolation. Never saw the surface world until the evacuation, learning about the outside only through stories and old Brotherhood records.

Unfortunately, [PAWN_pronoun] wasnâ€™t among the brightest and most gifted children.

[PAWN_nameDef] grew up in a settlement where only children lived. The other kids looked up to [PAWN_objective], both figurately and literally, as [PAWN_pronoun] was one of the oldest children. After [PAWN_pronoun] reached a certain age [PAWN_pronoun] had to, like it was custom, leave the town and make a living someplace else.

[PAWN_nameDef] was part of the comparatively young but rapidly growing cult called â€œThe Children of Atomâ€. [PAWN_nameDef] was a gardener in a cooperative agricultural project. [PAWN_pronoun] and his fellow co-op members tended to a communal garden and evenly split the yield. [PAWN_nameDef] was a very curious child. [PAWN_pronoun] was fascinated with every aspect of the vast wasteland, perhaps more so than was good for [PAWN_objective]. The wasteland was, without question, an extremely dangerous place. However, many Adventurers like [PAWN_nameDef] dared to explore the ruins of the past. [PAWN_nameDef] was born in the â€œgreen jewelâ€ of the commonwealth wasteland, Diamond City. [PAWN_nameDef] operated a small diner next to a road bustling with trade. [PAWN_pronoun] was happy to provide a warm meal and a place to sleep to any paying customers, as long as they didnâ€™t start any trouble.
War and strife were commonplace, even after the apocalypse. [PAWN_nameDef] and [PAWN_possessive] family were refugees, fleeing from one of the countless conflicts that arose in the wasteland.

After taking a few jobs here and there, [PAWN_nameDef] decided that a settled life wasnâ€™t the right thing for [PAWN_objective]. Instead, [PAWN_pronoun] hit the road and faced the wasteland with nothing but the clothes on [PAWN_possessive] back and [PAWN_possessive] trusty guitar, wandering from town to town without a clear goal or destination in mind.

During [PAWN_possessive] youth, [PAWN_nameDef] worked as a drug courier for a syndicate of the local organised crime. [PAWN_nameDef] was raised by a devoted cult. The cult aimed to form the largely barren wasteland into a paradise of green splendour. Through some means, probably illegal or unsavoury in nature, [PAWN_nameDef]â€™s parents became wealthy enough to afford a lifestyle most wastelanders only could dream about. This also meant, that [PAWN_pronoun] never had to care for [PAWN_objective]self and abhors the thought of doing tasks which [PAWN_pronoun] deemed beneath [PAWN_objective]self.

There are many different chems in the wasteland, and [PAWN_nameDef] tried most of them at least once. [PAWN_nameDef] spent much of [PAWN_possessive] adult life in a tribal-like society. While [PAWN_pronoun] managed to adapt to [PAWN_possessive] new surroundings relatively well, [PAWN_pronoun] remained sceptical towards high-tech technology.

[PAWN_nameDef] once was a slave, liberated by an abolitionist group somewhere in the wasteland. Although [PAWN_nameDef] regained [PAWN_possessive] freedom, enslavement left its marks and made [PAWN_pronoun] unable to enact violence upon others.

[PAWN_nameDef] was born on the very frontier of human civilisation, such as it is. [PAWN_nameDef] grew up around a reinforced gas station. [PAWN_nameDef] made a living by hunting Geckos and other smaller animals of the wasteland for their meat and hides. [PAWN_pronoun] is not only a good hunter but also knows how to maintain [PAWN_possessive] equipment.

[PAWN_nameDef] was born and raised in the settlement of Goodneighbour in the commonwealth. The town was rough and [PAWN_nameDef] had to learn early in life who [PAWN_pronoun] could trust to avoid getting shanked. It was a tough youth, but it made [PAWN_objective] even tougher.

As a child, [PAWN_nameDef] once found pre-war magazines about the greaser culture and old holodisks with rock music. This proved to be quite the formative experience, as [PAWN_pronoun] never left this phase behind and continued to live [PAWN_possessive] life the greaser way. Keep it cool, Daddy-O!

[PAWN_nameDef] was born on a remote farmstead. Early on in [PAWN_possessive] youth, [PAWN_pronoun] began to dream about a life far away from the dull and demanding farm work. Soon after, [PAWN_nameDef] set out to make it in the big city.

In [PAWN_possessive] youth [PAWN_nameDef] found an old revolver while scavenging in some ruins. Inspired by tales of the gunslingers of the old world, [PAWN_pronoun] took up the iron and began practicing.

[PAWN_nameDef] was one of the few remaining experts on performance enhancing implants left in the world. This made [PAWN_objective] an incredibly valuable asset for [PAWN_possessive] community. [PAWN_nameDef] grew up around a shop operated by a group of mechanics. They quickly realized how gifted [PAWN_nameDef] was and took [PAWN_objective] in. [PAWN_nameDef] was born as the child of a wasteland mercenary. Regardless of the many larger and well-known political entities and groups which thrived throughout the wasteland, the majority of the humans living in post-war America were part of smaller, more or less independent settlements and groups. [PAWN_nameDef] has always had a talent for chemistry and decided to put it to good use by making booze. Life in the wasteland is tough and many of its inhabitants need an occasional pick-me-up and [PAWN_nameDef] was willing to provide itâ€¦ as long as they paid in caps.

[PAWN_nameDef] was born and raised on the streets of a settlement somewhere in the wasteland. From a very young age [PAWN_pronoun] had to take care of [PAWN_objective]self and the fact [PAWN_pronoun] made it to adulthood at all showed [PAWN_possessive] tenacity.

[PAWN_nameDef] makes [PAWN_possessive] living by selling apparel, clothing and armor to the people of the wastes. [PAWN_nameDef] was a born tribal of the Capitol Wasteland. Not much is known about [PAWN_nameDef] except a wild speculation that [PAWN_pronoun] is from Oasis. A small unassuming and quite curious settlement of near fanatical cultists.

Unusual for an inhabitant of the wasteland, [PAWN_nameDef] always had an eye for aesthetics. While most other wastelanders didnâ€™t mind living in ruined pre-war buildings, [PAWN_nameDef] preferred to decorate [PAWN_possessive] dwelling with pieces of art, either self-made or scavenged ones.

[PAWN_nameDef] took on the dangerous profession of hunting criminals in a largely lawless world. The transition between bounty hunter and mercenary was often smooth and unclear, but [PAWN_pronoun] to think of [PAWN_objective]self as a bringer of law, judge, jury and executioner.

Like [PAWN_possessive] father, [PAWN_nameDef] became a butcher. Radroaches, molerats, squirrels, bloatflies and iguanas were the among the more affordable foodstuffs. Brahmin and bighorner meats were usually reserved for those with a refined palate. Unlike other aspects of life before the great war, like sanitation or education, prostitution remained a staple of post-war economy. While most smaller settlements in the wasteland were ruled over by the townâ€™s sheriff, some of the larger oneâ€™s had a mayor, who may or may not have been elected democratically. [PAWN_nameDef] was one of those mayors and while [PAWN_pronoun] enjoyed the benefits of the office, [PAWN_pronoun] deeply cared for the members of [PAWN_possessive] community.

In a world were few had the time and means to educate themselves, [PAWN_nameDef] took it upon [PAWN_objective]self to teach the children of [PAWN_possessive] community how to write, read and count as well as anything else [PAWN_pronoun] could find old books about. As much of the wasteland is arid and irradiated, clean water is a precious commodity. [PAWN_nameDef] worked as a merchant specialized in trading water, transporting it from its source to the thirsty settlements of the wasteland.

The hostility of the wasteland and many of its inhabitants, both beasts and humans, make the ability to craft and maintain weaponry indispensable. [PAWN_nameDef] has this gift and used it to look after the equipment of [PAWN_possessive] community.

Managed the supply lines and resources that kept the army moving. Skilled in organization, inventory, and problem-solving, this specialist ensured troops had the equipment and provisions needed for survival and victory.

Tasked with overseeing battalion-level operations, this major coordinated complex missions and managed both officers and enlisted personnel. Their strategic thinking and organizational skills were vital in the pre-war military machine.

Trained to fly the advanced Vertibird transport and gunship, this pilot mastered both troop deployment and aerial combat. Their quick reflexes and nerves of steel made them invaluable in high-risk missions.

Rose through the ranks of government to become President, leading with charisma and unwavering conviction. Devoted to the dream of restoring America to its former glory, no matter the cost. A master of rhetoric and political maneuvering.

Selected from among the nation's finest law enforcement personnel, this agent was trained in presidential protection, counterterrorism, and threat assessment. Their duties demanded constant vigilance, split-second decision-making, and the willingness to take a bullet for their assigned protectee. Cold professionalism defined their every action.

Handpicked from the Secret Service's most elite agents, this operative was trained to pilot the experimental T-65 power armor. Their missions involved critical national security operations that required overwhelming force and absolute discretion. Only the best of the best ever saw the inside of a T-65 suit, and they were expected to succeed where entire platoons would fail.

Expert in explosives and breaching operations, this Marine was responsible for clearing obstacles and sabotaging enemy positions. Their technical skills and nerves of steel were vital in high-risk missions.
Served as a rifleman in the United States Marine Corps, trained in amphibious assaults and close-quarters combat. Known for discipline, adaptability, and courage under fire.

Issued an experimental prototype of submersible Marine armor aboard the USS Democracy, this Marine kept watch within the boatâ€™s tight corridors and led boarding and security operations below the waves. Hardened by claustrophobic sea duty and brutal close-quarters combat, they relied on the armorâ€™s plating and specialized systems to repel raiders, lead boarding parties, and carry out dangerous missions along irradiated coastal and underwater sites.

The Brotherhood of Steel maintains strict military discipline and a reverence for pre-war technology. Members follow the Codex, value the chain of command, and see themselves as preservers of knowledge in a barbaric world.

The Outcasts are purists who rejected Elder Lyons' deviation from the Codex. They are utterly dedicated to acquiring and preserving technology, viewing outsiders as obstacles at best. The mission is everything.

Elder Lyons' chapter broke from the Codex to help the people of the Capital Wasteland. His followers combine Brotherhood discipline with compassion, believing that technology should serve humanity, not be hoarded from it.

The Enclave represents the remnants of the pre-war United States government, embodying a culture of technological superiority, strict hierarchy, and unwavering loyalty to their vision of a purified America. Members of the Enclave are deeply committed to their cause, often viewing themselves as the rightful inheritors of the Earth.
Culture of the Great Khans, a nomadic raider tribe descended from pre-war biker gangs.
Culture of those in the New California Republic.
Culture of those in the People's Republic of China.
Culture of those in the Raider Gangs.
Culture of those in the United States of America.
Survivors of the post-apocalyptic wasteland, scrapers and scavengers trying to rebuild civilization from the ruins.

These grotesque mutated rodents live underground in vast tunnel networks. They breed rapidly and swarm in large numbers when disturbed. Originally simple wasteland vermin, their population has exploded in underground areas where they create extensive burrow systems. They are aggressive, territorial, and attack anything that enters their domain.

The Brotherhood of Steel is one of the oldest still existing factions in the wasteland. It was founded just before and during the great war itself. True to its army origin, the Brotherhood is a militarised society, based on discipline, prowess, and reliability. However, there are a great number of Brotherhood chapters with often strongly deviating philosophies. Some might see themselves as the sole protectors of humanity and act noble and chivalrous, while others might see outsiders as clueless degenerates unfit for the technological marvels they might possess. Among the few things shared by all chapters of the Brotherhood is their obsession with technology (and the safeguarding of it) and their imposing suits of power armour which make them, despite their small numbers, one of the most powerful groups in the wasteland.

The Appalachia Chapter stands at a crossroads. Cut off from Lost Hills and forced to operate independently, you must decide your chapter's path: will you follow "The Chain That Binds" - the strict Codex doctrine that demands technological preservation above all else? Or will you break from tradition and become protectors of the wasteland's people, proving the Brotherhood can be more than collectors? Your isolation means no oversight, no reinforcements, but complete freedom to define what the Brotherhood truly stands for in these lands.

The Lost Hills Chapter is the original Brotherhood of Steel, founded by Roger Maxson in the ruins of the Mariposa Military Base. As the first and founding chapter, Lost Hills represents the purest form of the Brotherhood's ideals - technological preservation, military discipline, and isolationist philosophy. They are the keepers of the original Codex and consider themselves the supreme authority over all Brotherhood chapters. Their fortress in the Lost Hills bunker complex remains one of the most technologically advanced facilities in the wasteland, housing pre-war military hardware and the Brotherhood's most elite soldiers.

The Lyons' Pride remnants are a faction of disciplined and technologically advanced warriors, striving to preserve the ideals of their late leader, Elder Lyons. Once a proud and unified chapter, they now operate as a fragmented yet determined group, clinging to their mission of protecting humanity and safeguarding advanced technology. Though their numbers have dwindled, their resolve remains unshaken, and they continue to uphold their code of honor amidst the chaos of the wasteland. Their advanced weaponry, power armor, and tactical expertise make them a formidable force, whether as allies or adversaries.

Once a proud and unified chapter, they now operate as a fragmented yet determined group, clinging to their mission of protecting humanity and safeguarding advanced technology. Though their numbers have dwindled, their resolve remains unshaken, and they continue to uphold their code of honor amidst the chaos of the wasteland. Their advanced weaponry, power armor, and tactical expertise make them a formidable force, whether as allies or adversaries.

The Midwestern Brotherhood of Steel broke away from the main order during an airship expedition that crash-landed in the Midwest. Unlike their Western counterparts, the Midwestern chapter has adopted a more pragmatic and expansionist philosophy, actively recruiting tribals and wastelanders into their ranks. They maintain the Brotherhood's devotion to technology and military prowess, but have shed much of the isolationism that defines other chapters. Their territory spans vast stretches of the former American Midwest, making them one of the largest and most diverse Brotherhood chapters, though they remain estranged from Lost Hills' authority.

The Mojave Chapter of the Brotherhood of Steel is a shadow of its former self, forced out of the Hidden Valley bunker and now struggling to survive in the harsh Mojave wasteland. Once a proud and disciplined force, they have been reduced to a scattered group, clinging to their mission of safeguarding humanity's technological heritage. Despite their diminished state, their mastery of power armor and energy weapons ensures they remain a formidable presence in the region.

The Outcast Remnants are a hardened splinter group of the Brotherhood of Steel, formed by those who rejected the Brotherhood's changing ideals and chose to uphold the original mission: the recovery and preservation of advanced technology at any cost. Isolated from their former brothers, the Outcasts are fiercely independent, pragmatic, and often distrustful of outsiders. Their camps are fortified, their patrols relentless, and their loyalty to their cause unwavering. Though their numbers are few, their discipline, technical expertise, and mastery of power armor make them a formidable force in the wasteland.

Caesarâ€™s Legion is the fearsome group which conquered most of the former states of Arizona, New Mexico, Colorado, and Utah. The Legion styles itself after the ancient roman legionnaires and uses many low-tech weapons such as javelins and crude but effective melee weapons. They are cruel, warlike, and known to frequently commit massacres against civilians, which they view as dissolute and profligate. Caesar, or Edward Sallow as he was once known, leads his slave armies as an iron fisted dictator and pushes them ever forward to claim new territories for their leader, the self-proclaimed son of Mars himself. Before long, the Legion stood at the shores of the Colorado river and set their eyes on the gleaming towers of New Vegas and California beyond them.

Ruthless remnants of the old world government, the Enclave believe themselves to be humanityâ€™s rightful rulers. Isolated, technologically advanced, and fiercely nationalistic, they seek to restore civilization under their iron-fisted visionâ€”no matter the cost to outsiders.

You are operatives of the Enclave, the shadow government that considers itself the rightful heir to the United States of America. Armed with advanced pre-war technology and an unwavering belief in your genetic and technological superiority, you seek to restore America to its former glory - by any means necessary. The wasteland is filled with mutants and degenerates who have no place in the new America you will build.

The Great Khans are a tribal raider society that traces its roots back to the pre-war biker gangs of California. Once one of the most powerful raider tribes in the wasteland, they have endured numerous conflicts with the NCR and other factions that have decimated their numbers. Despite their setbacks, the Khans remain proud and fiercely independent, refusing to bow to anyone. They have become experts at producing chems, which they trade for weapons and supplies. The Khans value strength, loyalty to the tribe, and the freedom to live as they choose - free from the laws and taxes of "civilized" societies. They are a nomadic people, living in yurts and tents that can be quickly packed up when it's time to move on.

The New California Republic is a faction based out of Shady Sands in the former state of California. It is a federal parliamentary republic, similar to the pre-war USA before it descended into authoritarianism. They represent the old world and its values. Roads, taxes, prisons, elections, and other phenomena long forgotten made a comeback with the NCRâ€™s rise to power. It is seen by its citizens to be the sole haven of civilisation, democracy, and freedom in the wasteland. While there certainly is truth to that notion, the NCR also suffers from widespread corruption and oligarchic tendencies, for much power is held by exceedingly rich ranchers and farm owners. Furthermore, it uses its vast military, well supplied by their industrial centres, to aggressively expand their territory and claim the resources of neighbouring regions to sate the ever-growing hunger of its population.

A rogue faction of former NCR soldiers and officers who have deserted the Republic. Disillusioned with the NCR's leadership, they now operate as a hostile paramilitary group, surviving through raids and mercenary work. Their organization is loose, but they are led by a cunning Rogue Colonel.

The New California Republic is a democratic federation founded on the principles of law, order, and progress. As the NCR, you lead a colony striving to restore civilization and expand its influence across the wasteland.

Founded on ideals of collectivism, harmony, and technological advancement, the People's Republic of China is a nation of bustling cities, rich cultural heritage, and a strong sense of community. Beneath the surface, however, growing censorship, state control, and military expansion shape daily life. Citizens enjoy rapid modernization, vibrant traditions, and national pride, even as the specter of international conflict looms ever closer.

The Raider Gangs are a loose confederation of bandit groups operating in the wasteland. They are known for their brutal tactics, scavenging, and raiding of settlements. While they often fight among themselves, they can unite against common foes. The Raiders reject the old world values and instead embrace a survival-of-the-fittest mentality. They are feared and despised by many, but also hold a certain allure for those seeking freedom from the constraints of civilization.

Founded on ideals of freedom, prosperity, and technological progress, America is a nation of gleaming cities, suburban comfort, and boundless optimism. Beneath the surface, however, growing paranoia, corporate influence, and military escalation shaped daily life. Citizens enjoyed advanced conveniences, colorful pop culture, and patriotic pride, even as the shadow of nuclear war looms ever closer.

Small communities of wastelanders who have banded together to survive in the harsh post-war world. These settlers live in ramshackle towns, trading scrap and salvaged goods while trying to maintain some semblance of civilization. They're cautious but not hostile, willing to trade with strangers but always wary of raiders and other threats. Most are just trying to scrape by, growing mutated crops, herding brahmin, and defending what little they have.

The Brotherhood of Steel operates under the strict tenets of the Codex, a sacred doctrine that demands the preservation and control of advanced technology. Under Elder Maxson, the Brotherhood has united the ideals of both the Outcasts' technological zealotry and Lyons' willingness to engage with outsiders - but on the Brotherhood's terms. Technology must be controlled, mutants and synths are abominations to be destroyed, and only through discipline and hierarchy can humanity be saved from itself. The Brotherhood is both shield and sword - protecting humanity while ensuring dangerous technology never falls into the wrong hands.

Paladins are the Brotherhood's elite warriors, clad in power armor and wielding the most advanced weapons. They are the tip of the spear in any Brotherhood operation, trained to engage the most dangerous threats head-on. A Paladin's duty is to lead from the front, inspire their squadmates, and demonstrate the Brotherhood's martial superiority.

Lost Hills represents the original Brotherhood of Steel - the purest interpretation of the Codex as written by Roger Maxson himself. Technology must be preserved at any cost, outsiders are never to be trusted, and the Brotherhood's bloodlines must remain pure. Isolation is strength. The wasteland may burn, but the Brotherhood will endure, guarding humanity's technological legacy until the world is ready to rebuild - on Brotherhood terms.

The Shield of the People represents everything the Lyons Pride stands for - warriors who protect the innocent and fight for those who cannot fight for themselves. They combine Brotherhood combat prowess with genuine compassion, serving as both soldiers and humanitarian workers.

Elder Lyons taught that technology is worthless if it doesn't serve the people. The Lyons Pride believes the Brotherhood must be more than collectors and hoarders - they must be protectors and builders. While still bound by the Codex's technical wisdom, they reject its isolationism. Technology should be preserved, but also deployed to help those in need. The wasteland doesn't need another group of tech-hoarders; it needs guardians who understand that saving humanity means actually helping humans, not just safeguarding artifacts.

The Midwestern Brotherhood emerged from the wreckage of their crashed airships with a revolutionary understanding: survival demands adaptation. Unlike the isolationists at Lost Hills, the Midwest chapter actively recruits tribals, wastelanders, and even former enemies into their ranks. Technology still matters, but so does building an army capable of holding territory and protecting the people within it. The Codex is a guide, not a chain. Expansion is not corruption - it is evolution.

Defenders are Outcast specialists who combine superior technical knowledge with combat capability. They are responsible for securing technology from hostile forces and ensuring no advanced equipment falls into unworthy hands. Their dedication to the mission is absolute.

The Outcasts are the Brotherhood's purists - those who refused to compromise the Codex when Elder Lyons went soft. Technology is everything. It's not about helping wastelanders or building communities; it's about preserving humanity's technological legacy before fools destroy it all. The Old World fell because people misused technology - the Brotherhood's sacred duty is to prevent that from happening again by controlling every piece of advanced tech in existence. Outsiders are liabilities at best, threats at worst. Only through absolute dedication to the mission can the Brotherhood fulfill its purpose.
Our people should dominate all others.

The Enclave represents the continuation of the United States government, destined to reclaim and restore America. Only pure, untainted humans have the right to rebuild civilization. All others are aberrations to be removed or subjugated.

The Great Khans have perfected the art of chem production. Their chemists are revered members of the tribe, responsible for creating the drugs that fund the Khan's operations. A skilled chem maker is worth their weight in silver, and their knowledge is passed down through generations.

The Great Khans reject the chains of "civilized" society - no laws, no taxes, no rulers except the strongest among them. They value personal freedom above all else, but with that freedom comes responsibility to the tribe. Strength is respected, weakness is despised, and loyalty to your brothers and sisters is sacred. The Khans don't apologize for taking what they need - the wasteland belongs to those strong enough to claim it.

Once loyal to the New California Republic, these individuals have turned their backs on the old world order. Disillusioned by corruption and endless war, they now value survival, independence, and camaraderie above all else. They are wary of authority, skeptical of grand ideals, and quick to defend their own. Their code is one of mutual aid, hard-earned trust, and doing what it takes to survive in the wasteland.

The New California Republic (NCR) is a federal presidential republic founded in New California, comprised of five contiguous states and additional territorial holdings in Nevada and Baja California. The NCR emphasizes and strives to support a myriad of old world values, such as democracy, personal liberty, and the rule of law. It also aims to restore general order to the wasteland, the improvement and development of infrastructure and economic systems, and overarching peace between people. Similar to institutions of the old world it seeks to emulate, continued expansion has created challenges with territorial control, loyalty, and corruption that plague the Republic and serve to hinder its goals.

A volunteer special forces renowned as much for their expert reconnaissance capabilities as their bravery and combat prowess, the Rangers' primary purpose is dealing with threats the forces of the republic's standing Army cannot handle, as well as general peacekeeping around the borders of NCR territory, scouting out known and potential threats, and other special assignments. Their cutting-edge training, weaponry and assortment of both pre- and post-War technology often allows them to serve as lynchpins for victory in the republic's conflicts even when a situation may see them outnumbered and outgunned.

The Year of the Dragon ideology values national unity, collective effort, and technological progress. It encourages loyalty to the state, respect for tradition, and pride in cultural heritage. Citizens are expected to work together for the good of the nation, maintain social harmony, and embrace innovation, believing that unity and determination will bring prosperity and strength.

A shining promise of prosperity, freedom, and boundless opportunity, the American Dream fuels the hopes of millions across the nation. In these years, it is a vision of white-picket fences, two-car garages, and a future powered by atomic energy. Families gather around glowing televisions, confident that hard work and patriotism secure their place in a world of endless progress. Optimism runs high, and the Dream endures as a beacon of what America can achieveâ€”an unwavering belief in a brighter tomorrow. Yet beneath the optimism, cracks formâ€”corporate greed, resource shortages, and the shadow of war. Still, the Dream endures, a beacon of what America is meant to be, even as the world teeters on the edge of oblivion.

Shields combine combat prowess with genuine compassion, serving as both warriors and protectors. They embody Elder Lyons' belief that power must be used to defend those who cannot defend themselves.
Defenders are Outcast specialists who excel at both securing technology and eliminating threats. They combine technical expertise with ruthless efficiency, ensuring the mission succeeds at any cost.
Paladins are the Brotherhood's finest warriors, masters of power armor combat and tactical leadership. They lead squads into the most dangerous situations and serve as examples of martial excellence.
The Brotherhood's strength comes from discipline and hierarchy. Every member knows their place and their duty. Orders flow from Elder to Paladin to Knight to Initiate without question.

Advanced technology is too dangerous to be left in the hands of the unprepared. The Brotherhood has a sacred duty to preserve, control, and safeguard all pre-war technology to prevent another apocalypse.

The wasteland is full of threats - raiders, mutants, and fools who would misuse technology. The Brotherhood endures by keeping to itself, trading only when necessary, and never revealing our full strength to outsiders.

The Brotherhood's strength comes from its founding bloodlines. Those born into the Brotherhood carry the legacy of Roger Maxson and the original Mariposa survivors. Outsiders can never truly understand our mission.
Technology is a tool, not an end unto itself. The Brotherhood exists to serve humanity, not just preserve artifacts. Those with power have a duty to shield the weak.
protect those who cannot protect themselves

Territory means resources, recruits, and security. The Midwest Brotherhood does not hide in bunkers - we claim land, build outposts, and protect the people within our borders. A Brotherhood that does not grow will eventually die.

The Brotherhood cannot survive on bloodlines alone. Tribals, wastelanders, even former enemies - anyone with skill and dedication can earn their place in the Brotherhood. Recruitment is not weakness; it is wisdom.
The Brotherhood cannot trust wastelanders with technology or secrets. Outsiders lack discipline, understanding, and loyalty. They're resources at best, threats at worst.
Nothing is more important than recovering and preserving pre-war technology. Not wastelander lives, not politics, not sentiment. The mission is everything.
An elite officer of the Enclave, trained in leadership and advanced combat tactics. They excel at research, combat, and maintaining discipline.

Khan chemists are the lifeblood of the tribe's economy. They produce the chems that the Khans trade for weapons, supplies, and respect. A master chemist is valued as highly as the tribe's greatest warriors.

The Rangers, especially the Veteran Rangers, have a fearsome reputation with friend and foe alike. In the Mojave, posters can be seen of them and some NCR troopers believe they "chew nails and spit napalm." Even men of Caesar's Legion speak of the Rangers with respect, calling them "the best the profligates have to offer."

The Communist Party Leader is the highest authority in the People's Republic of China, representing the core values of the party and the nation. This role is responsible for guiding the people, maintaining unity, and advancing the country's collective goals and progress.

The Governor is the elected leader of the colony, embodying the ideals of prosperity, freedom, and progress. Responsible for guiding citizens, upholding American values, and ensuring a brighter future for all.

The Elder is the supreme commander of a Brotherhood chapter, responsible for all strategic decisions, diplomatic relations, and the chapter's overall direction. They have devoted their lives to the Brotherhood's cause and earned the respect of all members through wisdom, skill, and unwavering dedication to preserving technology and protecting humanity.

The lowest rank in the Brotherhood of Steel. Initiates are in training, learning the Brotherhood's ways and proving their worth. They perform basic duties and assist full members while earning the right to become a true Brother or Sister of Steel.

Knights are the Brotherhood's front-line warriors, fully trained in combat operations and the maintenance of power armor. They lead patrols, engage hostile forces, and recover pre-War technology from dangerous locations. Knights are the backbone of Brotherhood military strength.

Paladins are the Brotherhood's elite warriors and field commanders. They lead major operations, command Knights in battle, and make critical tactical decisions. Clad in the finest power armor and wielding the most advanced weapons, Paladins embody the Brotherhood's military might and tactical expertise.

Scribes are the Brotherhood's scholars and technicians, dedicated to preserving and understanding pre-War technology. They maintain archives, research artifacts, and ensure that knowledge is not lost. While not primarily combatants, Scribes are crucial to the Brotherhood's mission.

The leader of Lyons' Pride remnants, carrying on Elder Lyons' legacy through action rather than doctrine. They have chosen compassion over dogma, humanity over tradition, and remain the last true believers in Elder Lyons' dream of a Brotherhood that serves rather than rules.
Full members of Lyons' Pride who have proven themselves worthy of Elder Lyons' trust. They fight to protect the innocent and represent the best of what the Brotherhood can be when it serves humanity.

New members of Lyons' Pride who have sworn to fight for Elder Lyons' vision of a protected Capital Wasteland. Recruits are proving their dedication to defending humanity, not just hoarding technology.

Elite warriors who embody Elder Lyons' vision completely. They are the shield between wasteland communities and the dangers that threaten them, fighting not for glory or technology, but for the survival of humanity itself.

Defenders are the Outcasts' combat specialists, focused on securing technology and defending Outcast installations. They reject the Brotherhood's shift toward humanitarianism, maintaining strict focus on their original mission.

The Protector leads the Outcasts, maintaining iron discipline and unwavering commitment to technology preservation. They represent the purest form of the Brotherhood's original mission, refusing any compromise or deviation from the founder's vision.

The entry rank for Outcasts. Specialists have proven their commitment to the original Brotherhood mission of technology recovery above all else. They serve the Outcasts' pragmatic, no-nonsense approach to preserving pre-War knowledge.

Senior field officers who command company-sized units. Captains are proven leaders with extensive combat experience. They handle complex operations and serve as the Enclave's primary tactical commanders.

High-ranking officers who oversee strategic operations and base management. Colonels answer directly to the Enclave leadership and are responsible for executing the organization's broader objectives. They command respect and absolute authority.

Junior officers responsible for platoon-level operations. Lieutenants coordinate multiple squads and make tactical decisions in the field. They are expected to demonstrate both combat prowess and leadership ability.

The lowest rank in the Enclave military structure. Privates are frontline soldiers, trained in the use of advanced weaponry and power armor. They carry out orders without question and serve as the backbone of Enclave forces.

Non-commissioned officers who lead small squads in the field. Sergeants are experienced soldiers who maintain discipline and coordinate tactical operations. They bridge the gap between officers and enlisted personnel.

The highest rank that achievable in the New California Republic without a political ally. This is one of the highest command positions with the ability to call in reinforcements at the company level and higher including special forces.

The first rank within the New California Republic's commissioned officer corps. This is the lowest command position with the ability to call in reinforcements and supplies much faster and with greater quality and quantity.
The lowest rank within the New California Republic's military. There isn't a lot of repect in this role, nor is the pay well.
The first rank within the New California Republic's non commissioned officer corps. This is the first step in a professional military carrer with reaL power and responsibilities.

The highest rank that achievable in the United States Army without a political ally. This is one of the highest command positions with the ability to call in reinforcements at the company level and higher including special forces.

The first rank within the United States Army's commissioned officer corps. This is the lowest command position with the ability to call in reinforcements and supplies much faster and with greater quality and quantity.
The lowest rank within the United States Army. There isn't a lot of respect in this role, nor is the pay well.
The first rank within the United States' non commissioned officer corps. This is the first step in a professional military carrer with reaL power and responsibilities.
The lowest rank within the United States Marine Corps. There isn't a lot of respect in this role, nor is the pay well.
The first rank within the United States Marine Corps' non commissioned officer corps. This is the first step in a professional military career with real power and responsibilities.
Call for Brotherhood of Steel food supplies.
Call for Brotherhood of Steel technical resources and components.
Call for Brotherhood of Steel standard uniforms.
Call for Brotherhood of Steel standard issue weapons.
Call for Enclave power armor deployment.
Call for an Enclave supply drop containing survival essentials.
Call for advanced technology and components from Enclave stores.
Call for advanced Enclave weaponry including plasma weapons.
Call for NCR Ranger Veteran Armor.

You were part of the Brotherhood's Appalachian expedition, sent to track down rogue members and secure the region's abundant pre-war military installations. The mission went sideways when the Scorched plague decimated your forces.\n\nNow you're the survivors of a failed chapter, carrying the weight of lessons learned the hard way. You've seen what happens when the Brotherhood spreads too thin, when ideology clashes with survival. Time to start fresh, build smart, and maybe do things differently this time.

You are the last survivors of Elder Lyons' reformed Brotherhood - those who believed in protecting the people, not just hoarding technology. After the reunification and return to orthodox doctrine, you were ostracized for your "wasteful" compassion.\n\nFleeing the Capital Wasteland, you seek to establish a new chapter that honors Lyons' vision: a Brotherhood that serves humanity, not just preserves the past. You carry less equipment than a standard Brotherhood deployment, but your ideals remain unshaken.

Your squad was dispatched from Lost Hills to investigate reports of advanced pre-war technology in this region. The vertibird crashed during a storm, scattering your equipment across the wasteland. As knights and scribes of the Brotherhood of Steel, your mission is clear: secure the technology, establish a forward operating base, and bring order to the chaos.\n\nThe Brotherhood's tenets guide you - preserve technology, maintain discipline, and never forget the mistakes of the past.

You are the survivors of the Hidden Valley bunker, the remnants of a once-proud Brotherhood chapter brought low by Elder Elijah's obsession and the NCR's relentless pressure. After years of isolation and dwindling resources, your bunker has finally been compromised. Scattered and desperate, you've emerged into the wasteland with what little equipment you could salvage. The old Brotherhood doctrine of technological preservation seems like a luxury when survival is uncertain. Can you rebuild the Mojave chapter's strength, or will you fade into the desert like so many others before you?

You are the Outcasts - the true believers who refused to abandon the Codex when Elder Lyons went soft. While he wasted resources protecting wastelanders, you remained dedicated to the Brotherhood's real mission: acquiring and preserving technology.\n\nAfter years of conflict and eventual reunification on unfavorable terms, you've struck out to find a place where the Codex is still law. You start with superior equipment and technical knowledge, but your hardline stance makes diplomacy... challenging.

You are operatives of the Enclave, the shadow government of pre-war America. Armed with advanced technology and an unwavering belief in genetic superiority, you've been tasked with establishing a new outpost to further the Enclave's vision of a pure America. The wasteland is filled with mutants and degenerates - it is your duty to cleanse it.

Your outpost was attacked by unknown enemies. Your squad was sent off in the dead of night to get reinforcements, but you have now become lost. Will you work your way back to the NCR? Or will you stay where you are and survive as new citizens of this desolate wasteland?

Stranded behind enemy lines after a failed operation, your infantry platoon must rely on teamwork and resourcefulness to survive. Cut off from support and surrounded by hostile forces, you must navigate dangerous territory, manage scarce supplies, and find a way to safety.
A location with a dangerous glowing one that could potentially be captured.
A location with an extremely dangerous, berserk glowing one.
A prisoner being held by enemies.
A prisoner being held by enemies.

Colonel Autumn of the Enclave leads with military precision and overwhelming technological force. His doctrine emphasizes relentless pressure, mechanized warfare, and genetic superiority. Threats arrive in coordinated waves with minimal recovery time, forcing you to maintain constant combat readiness. Expect mechanoid incursions, genetic manipulation opportunities, and diseases that test colony resilience. This is war, and Autumn doesn't negotiate with inferior specimens.

A brilliant but utterly mad scientist obsessed with pre-war technology and utterly contemptuous of human life. Elijah sees people as tools - expendable pieces in his grand designs. He'll isolate you, trap you with scarcity and danger, and force you to raid ancient ruins for treasures. He's methodical and calculating, layering multiple threats while cutting you off from easy escape. Let go - but don't expect mercy. Finding the gravship is everything.

A veteran Desert Ranger who's survived everything the Mojave could throw at him through a mix of skill, grit, and sheer dumb luck. Rick brings a ranger's perspective to colony leadership - constant vigilance, regular patrols (threats), and the importance of strong faction relationships. Expect steady pressure balanced with frequent caravans and diplomatic opportunities. He'll join your colony personally if you prove worthy through a special quest. The wasteland is tough, but rangers are tougher.

The schizophrenic Nightkin leader from Black Mountain now narrates your colony's fate through her fractured consciousness. Rhonda, her imaginary Mr. Handy companion, whispers dark truths about things lurking beyond perception. Tabitha's episodes create wildly erratic threat patterns - days of supernatural horror followed by unexplained calm, with entities and anomalies that defy wasteland logic. Her madness sees connections others cannot, drawing forth eldritch phenomena and twisted experiments. Best Friends forever!

The ever-present mascot of Vault-Tec Corporation, representing the true purpose behind the vaults - controlled experiments on human populations. Unlike his optimistic counterpart, Vault Boy's corporate algorithms favor rapid testing and variable conditions. Expect shorter cycles between threats with occasional surprise double incidents, mixed with frequent opportunities for trade and social development. Every settlement is just another data point in the grand experiment.

She emerged from Vault 84 with technical manuals and a optimistic smile, believing every settlement can thrive with proper preparation. Her experience in controlled vault environments means she understands the balance between challenge and survival - threats come in measured waves with breathing room between, giving you time to fortify, heal, and prepare. She'll test you with raiders and wasteland creatures, but she wants you to succeed.
Heavy-duty metal flooring with round ventilation grills, commonly found in pre-war military bunkers. Durable and easy to clean.
Heavy-duty metal flooring with straight ventilation grills, commonly found in pre-war military bunkers. Durable and easy to clean.

The largest and most dangerous deathclaws rise to dominance through combat. Alpha males are massive predators with thicker hide and more powerful attacks, while matriarch females are the mothers of the pack and lay eggs to continue the species. Both are incredibly deadly and nearly impossible to tame.

A coastal variant of the mirelurk that has adapted to sandy beach environments. These creatures have lighter-colored shells that blend with beach sand, making them harder to spot until they emerge from the surf. Beachlurks are slightly more aggressive than their swamp-dwelling cousins and often bury themselves in sand to ambush prey.

A mutated descendant of bighorn sheep. These hardy creatures are commonly used as pack animals and livestock throughout the wasteland, valued for their strength, meat, and hide. Males sport impressive curved horns and are larger and more aggressive than females.

A large, agile, and powerful creature with deadly claws, the deathclaw is the most fearsome predator of the wasteland. Created through pre-war genetic engineering, these apex predators are incredibly dangerous and nearly impossible to tame. They hunt in packs and can tear through armor with ease.

A dangerous mutated gecko with the ability to breathe fire. These fearsome creatures have developed specialized glands that allow them to expel flames at their enemies. Fire geckos are highly aggressive and territorial, making them extremely dangerous to encounter in the wild. Their unique physiology makes them resistant to heat and fire, and their hide is particularly valuable. They are typically found in hot, desert regions.

A large mutated gecko that has grown to the size of a small dog. These reptilian creatures are common throughout the wasteland, particularly in desert regions. While not particularly aggressive, they will defend themselves when threatened. Their meat is edible and their hide makes decent leather.

Some dogs are wild, plaguing the wastes and killing livestock, but tame versions are necessary for many struggling wastelanders. There are also rare cases of domestic breeds that act as companions towards their bipedal counterparts. Humans and super mutants are the only species known to truly tame and domesticate these canines.

A heavily mutated praying mantis grown to enormous size by prolonged radiation exposure. These aggressive predators hunt with lightning-fast reflexes and devastating raptorial forelegs capable of impaling prey in a single strike. Their chitinous exoskeleton provides considerable protection, and they're known to be territorial and highly aggressive when threatened. Found primarily in arid regions where they ambush unsuspecting victims.

A radroach that has absorbed extreme amounts of radiation, causing it to emit an eerie green glow. These mutants are more aggressive than their common cousins and their bodies are saturated with radioactive isotopes. Exposure to them can be hazardous, but their glowing carapaces have some value to certain traders.

A heavily irradiated radstag that emits an eerie green glow. Prolonged exposure to radiation has made these creatures larger and more aggressive than normal radstags. Their meat is highly radioactive and dangerous to consume, but their glowing presence makes them easy to spot at night.

A large mutated gecko with distinctive golden scales that shimmer in the light. These creatures are prized for their valuable hide, which can be crafted into high-quality leather goods. Golden geckos are stronger and more aggressive than their common cousins, and their bite can deliver a mild poison. They are found in desert regions and rocky terrain.

A large mutated gecko with green scales and a more aggressive temperament than common geckos. Green geckos are tougher and more dangerous, with a stronger bite that can deliver toxins. They are commonly found in rocky areas and caves throughout the wasteland. Their hide is valuable and their meat is edible, though it must be properly prepared.

A highly evolved mutant creature that combines features of snapping turtles and cuttlefish. Unlike mirelurks, lakelurks are much more aggressive and possess a sonic attack that can disorient prey. Their shells are extremely tough, and they're far more intelligent than other lurk variants. Found primarily in large lakes and deep waters, they're apex aquatic predators of the wasteland.

A massive male lakelurk that has grown to enormous proportions. Lakelurk kings are territorial apex predators that claim entire lakes as their domain. They possess enhanced sonic abilities that can stun multiple targets, incredibly thick shells, and devastating strength. These solitary giants are always male and highly aggressive, attacking anything that enters their territory. Extremely rare and highly dangerous.

Some dogs are wild, plaguing the wastes and killing livestock, but tame versions are necessary for many struggling wastelanders. There are also rare cases of domestic breeds such as German Shepherds act as companions towards their bipedal counterparts. The Minutemen around the ruins of Boston in particular have used them as working and guard dogs for their military.

A heavily mutated crustacean that has grown to enormous size. These creatures have thick, chitinous shells that provide excellent protection against most weapons. Mirelurks are territorial and aggressive, using their powerful claws to defend their nesting grounds. They're typically found near bodies of water and coastal areas, scavenging for food and defending their territory with surprising ferocity.

Despite its name, the mirelurk king is not actually a mirelurk at all. This towering bipedal predator is believed to be a heavily mutated Jackson's chameleon, sharing no direct relation to the crustacean-like mirelurks. The name comes from their tendency to inhabit areas near mirelurk colonies. Kings stand on two legs, possess powerful clawed hands, and can emit devastating sonic screams. Their exposed flesh and lack of a protective shell is compensated by remarkable agility and deadly close-range attacks.

A grotesque mutated rodent that has grown to an unusually large size. These hairless, aggressive creatures are common throughout the wasteland, particularly in underground areas and ruins. They travel in packs and will attack anything that enters their territory. Their meat is barely edible and their hide is nearly worthless, but they breed rapidly and can overwhelm unprepared travelers.

A horrifying product of pre-war genetic experimentation, the nightstalker is a hybrid creature combining the DNA of coyotes and rattlesnakes. These stealthy predators hunt in packs under cover of darkness, using their venomous bite to bring down prey. They possess the pack intelligence of canines combined with the stealth and venom of serpents, making them one of the wasteland's most feared predators. Their distinctive silhouette and glowing eyes strike terror into those who encounter them.

A rare albino variant of the radstag, with pale white fur and lighter build. These creatures are less affected by radiation than their common cousins, making them slightly weaker but also less dangerous to handle. They're prized for their distinctive appearance.
A common sight on wasteland farms and ranches as well as small towns. It can grow fast and be farmed for eggs, meat and the occasional feather.

A mutated rabbit that has adapted to the radioactive wasteland. These creatures are significantly larger than their pre-war ancestors and have developed a surprising resilience to radiation. They're skittish but breed rapidly, making them a common sight across the wasteland. Despite their exposure to radiation, their meat is relatively safe to eat and they can be easily domesticated.

A clever and opportunistic scavenger that has thrived in the wasteland. These large black birds are known for their intelligence and adaptability, often following travelers and raiders to feed on carrion. While they prefer scavenging, rad ravens are capable predators that hunt small creatures. They can be trained to a useful degree, making them valuable companions for wasteland scouts.

A hardy seabird that has adapted remarkably well to the irradiated coasts of the wasteland. These gregarious birds travel in large flocks, their distinctive calls echoing across contaminated shorelines. Radgulls are highly resistant to radiation and can eat almost anything, making them successful scavengers. They're commonly found along coastal areas where they dive for mutated fish and pick through debris. With intermediate intelligence, they can be trained for various tasks.

A mutated cockroach, grown to enormous size by radiation exposure. Radroaches are highly adaptive pests that can survive in nearly any environment. While individually weak, they reproduce rapidly and can swarm unwary victims. Their meat is edible, though not particularly appetizing.

A mutated two-headed deer, one of the more common creatures in the wasteland. Despite their unusual appearance, radstags are generally docile herbivores that travel in small herds. Their meat is edible and their hides make decent leather, though both carry traces of radiation.

A freshwater variant of the mirelurk found primarily in rivers and streams. These creatures have darker shells that help them blend into murky river bottoms. Riverlurks are somewhat more docile than their coastal relatives but still dangerous when threatened. They prefer fast-moving water and are excellent swimmers.

A mutated, aggressive turkey that survived the nuclear holocaust. These irradiated birds are larger and more dangerous than their pre-war ancestors, often attacking on sight. They've adapted well to the wasteland, thriving in various environments.

Vicious dogs are a nearly hairless and apparently, partially ghoulified species of wasteland canine. Ruthless survivors of the wastes, these starving, irradiated mutts will attack anything that moves.

Vicious dogs are a nearly hairless and apparently, partially ghoulified species of wasteland canine. Ruthless survivors of the wastes, these starving, irradiated mutts will attack anything that moves. Some dogs have been domesticated, even charged with guarding its owner and all their property this animal will do so to the best of its ability.

A terrifying mutated bear, far larger and more aggressive than its pre-war ancestors. The name "Yao Guai" comes from the Chinese word for demon, and these apex predators live up to their fearsome reputation. Their thick hide provides excellent protection, and their ferocity makes them one of the most dangerous creatures in the wasteland.
A fertilized beachlurk egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into a beachlurk. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
An unfertilized beachlurk egg. These pale, sandy-colored eggs are found in coastal nests. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.

A fertilized deathclaw egg. If properly incubated, this will hatch into one of the most dangerous predators in the wasteland. These eggs are extremely rare and highly sought after by beast masters and scientists alike. It can be eaten, but most would consider that a terrible waste.
An unfertilized deathclaw egg. These massive eggs are incredibly rare and valuable. While they can be eaten, most would consider it a waste of such a valuable commodity.

A fertilized fire gecko egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into a fire gecko. These eggs are notably warm and must be kept at high temperatures to hatch. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
An unfertilized fire gecko egg. These eggs are warm to the touch. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
A fertilized gecko egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into a gecko. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
An unfertilized gecko egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
A fertilized golden gecko egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into a golden gecko. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
An unfertilized golden gecko egg. These eggs have a distinctive shimmer. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
A fertilized green gecko egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into a green gecko. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
An unfertilized green gecko egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
A fertilized lakelurk egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into a lakelurk. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
An unfertilized lakelurk egg. These large, dark eggs from the apex predators of irradiated lakes are rare and valuable. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
A fertilized mirelurk egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into a mirelurk. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
