An unfertilized mirelurk egg. These large, slimy eggs are a delicacy in some wasteland settlements. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
A fertilized Rad Chicken egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into a chick. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
An unfertilized Rad Chicken egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
A fertilized Rad Raven egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into a chick. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
An unfertilized Rad Raven egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
A fertilized Radgull egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into a chick. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
An unfertilized Radgull egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
A fertilized radroach egg. If incubated, it will hatch into a radroach nymph. These pests breed rapidly, and their eggs are a common sight in infested areas.
An unfertilized radroach egg. Small and unappetizing, but edible in a pinch. Radroaches lay eggs in large clusters.
A fertilized riverlurk egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into a riverlurk. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
An unfertilized riverlurk egg. These greenish eggs are found in freshwater nests. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
A fertilized thrasher egg. If properly incubated, it will hatch into an aggressive rad turkey. While they can be domesticated, their temperamental nature makes them dangerous livestock.
An unfertilized thrasher egg. Larger and more flavorful than pre-war turkey eggs, though some find the slightly irradiated taste off-putting. Still, it's a valuable food source in the wasteland.
An active mole rat burrow. If left alone, it will spawn additional mole rats and spread more burrows throughout the area. The rodents will defend their territory aggressively.

Raw meat from wasteland beasts. The flesh is tougher and has a slightly off taste compared to domesticated animals, bearing the marks of radiation and harsh conditions. Suitable for meals, though it may contain trace amounts of wasteland contamination.

Raw meat harvested from mutated wasteland insects. The flesh has an unusual texture and a faintly bitter taste. While nutritious, the high concentration of strange compounds from the insect's diet of irradiated materials makes it somewhat toxic.

Firm, white meat harvested from wasteland crustaceans. The flesh is dense and flavorful, though it tends to absorb waterborne contaminants from the creature's aquatic habitat. Popular among coastal survivors despite the mild radiation.

Lean meat from wasteland reptiles and amphibians. The flesh is stringy but edible, with a slightly gamey flavor. These creatures are particularly adept at storing radiation in their tissues, making the meat more contaminated than most.

Exceptionally fat and juicy meat from well-fed mutant insects. The tissue is swollen with nutritious compounds, making it more palatable than typical bug meat. Wasteland survivors prize these cuts, though they still carry moderate toxicity from the creature's irradiated diet.

Exceptionally meaty cuts from well-fed wasteland birds. The breast meat is thick and juicy, rivaling pre-war poultry in quality. Despite the radiation, this is considered a premium food source in the wasteland.

Meat from mutated wasteland birds. The flesh is recognizable as poultry but has an off-color tint and unusual smell from radiation exposure. While still edible and familiar in texture, it's clearly not from any pre-war chicken.

Exceptionally high-quality meat from a healthy, well-fed wasteland beast. The muscle tissue is well-marbled and surprisingly clean considering its origins. Prime cuts like these are rare finds and command premium prices. Still carries minimal radiation exposure from the wasteland environment.

Superior quality reptilian meat from a well-developed specimen. The muscle tissue is firm and properly developed, offering better texture and flavor than typical herptile meat. Even prime specimens carry moderate radiation from their wasteland diet.

Surprisingly good meat from a healthy wasteland varmint. The animal was well-fed and its meat is tender despite the small size. These cuts are prized by survivors who know how to prepare them properly. Still moderately radioactive.

Delicate, flavorful meat from wasteland fish. Its texture is tender and moist, making it a rare delicacy among survivors. While highly nutritious, it may still carry trace contaminants from irradiated waters.

Small game meat from wasteland rodents and other vermin. The portions are meager and the meat quality is poor, but beggars can't be choosers. These scavengers accumulate significant radiation from eating contaminated food scraps.
The 10mm Pistol is a semi-automatic sidearm chambered for 10mm rounds. Favored for its reliability and stopping power, it is a common sight among wastelanders and pre-war security forces alike.
An improved version of the classic 10mm Pistol, featuring enhanced reliability and accuracy. Favored by elite wastelanders and pre-war security forces.

The 10mm SMG is a compact, fully automatic submachine gun chambered for 10mm rounds. Known for its high rate of fire and ease of use, it is ideal for close-quarters combat but sacrifices accuracy at longer ranges.

The 12.7mm Pistol is a heavy, semi-automatic sidearm chambered for 12.7mm rounds. Its immense stopping power and armor penetration make it a favorite among wasteland enforcers, but its weight and recoil require a strong hand.

The 12.7mm SMG is a powerful, fully automatic submachine gun chambered for 12.7mm rounds. Known for its high damage output and rapid rate of fire, it is devastating in close-quarters combat but suffers from heavy recoil and limited accuracy at range.
The 14mm Pistol is a powerful sidearm chambered for 14mm rounds, offering exceptional stopping power and penetration. Rare and highly sought after in the wasteland.
The .223 Pistol is a unique sidearm chambered for .223 rifle rounds, offering high velocity and accuracy. Its design is favored by marksmen and collectors.

The .223 Rangemaster is a precision bolt-action rifle chambered for .223 rounds. Favored by marksmen for its high accuracy and range, it is a reliable choice for hunting and long-range engagements in the wasteland.
The .223 Rifle is a lightweight, semi-automatic rifle chambered for .223 rounds. Known for its accuracy and low recoil, it is a popular choice for both hunting and combat in the wasteland.

The .32 Hunting Rifle is a pre-war bolt-action rifle chambered for .32 caliber rounds. Known for its simplicity and reliability, it is a common weapon among wastelanders, offering decent accuracy and stopping power for medium-range engagements.

The .357 Revolver is a classic, six-shot sidearm chambered for .357 Magnum rounds. Renowned for its reliability and stopping power, it is a favored weapon among lawmen and gunslingers in the wasteland.

The .44 Magnum Revolver is a powerful, six-shot sidearm chambered for .44 Magnum rounds. Renowned for its high stopping power and intimidating presence, it is a prized weapon among wasteland hunters and enforcers.

The 5.56mm Pistol is a rare, high-powered sidearm chambered for 5.56mm rifle rounds. Its unique design offers superior penetration and range compared to typical pistols, but with increased recoil and wear.

The 9mm Pistol is a lightweight, semi-automatic sidearm chambered for 9mm rounds. Its low recoil and plentiful ammunition make it a popular choice for both civilians and wastelanders, though it lacks the stopping power of larger calibers.

The 9mm SMG is a lightweight, fully automatic submachine gun chambered for 9mm rounds. Known for its ease of use and high rate of fire, it is effective in close-quarters combat but lacks stopping power and accuracy at longer ranges.

A rare, limited edition BB gun crafted for the Abilene Kid. Distinguished by its unique markings and exceptional craftsmanship, it is prized by collectors and marksmen for its nostalgic value and precision.
The AK-112 is a robust, pre-war assault rifle chambered for 5.56mm rounds. Known for its reliability and ease of maintenance, it remains a popular choice among wasteland survivors.

All-American is a legendary marksman carbine renowned for its exceptional accuracy, reliability, and custom craftsmanship. Featuring a unique design and advanced optics, it excels at delivering precise, rapid bursts at long range, making it a prized weapon among elite marksmen and collectors alike.

A compact, fully automatic carbine chambered for 5mm rounds. Designed for military use, it offers a high rate of fire and excellent handling, making it ideal for close-quarters combat and suppressive fire.
A simple air-powered BB gun. While not lethal, it can sting and is often used for training or pest control. Not recommended for serious combat.

Big Boomer is a legendary, custom double-barrel shotgun renowned for its immense stopping power and rugged craftsmanship. Its reinforced frame and oversized barrels deliver devastating close-range blasts, making it a prized weapon among wasteland hunters and collectors alike.

The Brush Gun is a lever-action rifle chambered for .45-70 Government rounds. Favored for its high stopping power and reliability, it is a popular choice among wasteland hunters and marksmen for taking down large game and armored targets.
A double-barreled, break-action shotgun popular with caravan guards and traders. Its simple design makes it easy to repair and maintain, but it suffers from a slow reload and limited range
A rugged and powerful assault rifle of Chinese origin. It was widely used by Chinese forces during the Sino-American War, known for its high damage output and reliability in harsh conditions.

The Chinese Pistol is a compact, semi-automatic sidearm chambered for 9mm rounds. Manufactured before the Great War, it is lightweight and easy to conceal, but suffers from low stopping power and questionable reliability.
A massive, custom heavy weapon designed for maximum firepower and intimidation.
The Citykiller is a high-capacity, semi-automatic shotgun designed for urban combat. Its rapid fire and large magazine make it devastating in close quarters.
The Colt 6520 is a classic pre-war sidearm, renowned for its reliability and ease of use. Chambered for .45 rounds, it remains a favorite among wasteland survivors.

The standard combat rifle is a rugged, semi-automatic rifle chambered for .45 ACP rounds. Reliable and versatile, it is a favorite among wastelanders for its balance of firepower, accuracy, and durability.

The Combat Shotgun is a semi-automatic, magazine-fed shotgun chambered for 12 gauge shells. Designed for military and law enforcement, it offers a high rate of fire and reliability in close-quarters combat, making it a favorite among wasteland enforcers.
An advanced version of the Combat Shotgun, featuring improved reliability, accuracy, and rate of fire. Favored by elite military units and wasteland enforcers.
A classic lever-action rifle chambered for .357 rounds. The Cowboy Repeater is lightweight, reliable, and favored by marksmen and wanderers alike for its fast cycling and respectable accuracy.
The Deagle is a massive, semi-automatic pistol chambered for .50 AE rounds. Its intimidating size and firepower make it a symbol of status and strength in the wasteland.

Deliverer is a legendary, compact 10mm pistol renowned for its exceptional rate of fire and lightweight design. Favored by covert operatives, it features a custom suppressor and ergonomic grip, making it ideal for stealth operations and rapid engagements in close quarters.

Dinner Bell is a unique, custom pump-action shotgun prized for its exceptional reliability and stopping power. Featuring a polished finish and precision modifications, it delivers tight shot groupings and increased damage, making it the weapon of choice for seasoned wasteland hunters seeking both performance and style.
The DKS-501 is a pre-war bolt-action sniper rifle chambered for .308 rounds. Known for its exceptional accuracy and stopping power, it is a favorite among marksmen for long-range engagements.
The G11 is a revolutionary German assault rifle using caseless ammunition. Its high rate of fire and advanced design make it a rare but highly sought-after weapon in the wasteland.

The Gobi Campaign Scout Rifle is a legendary, custom sniper rifle awarded to elite marksmen. Renowned for its lightweight frame, exceptional accuracy, and rapid handling, it stands as one of the most coveted long-range weapons in the wasteland. Its unique craftsmanship and storied history make it a prized possession among sharpshooters and collectors alike.

A rugged, industrial nailgun adapted for both construction and combat. Its heavy frame and rapid-fire capability make it a formidable improvised weapon, favored by those who value versatility and raw stopping power.
The HK-10 is a modern, compact submachine gun known for its reliability and high rate of fire. Favored by security forces and elite units for urban operations.

The Hunting Revolver is a large, single-action revolver chambered for .45-70 Government rounds. Designed for hunting big game, it delivers devastating power and accuracy, but its heavy frame and slow rate of fire make it unwieldy in close combat.
A pump-action shotgun designed for hunting game. Favored for its rugged construction, high capacity, and reliability in the harshest wasteland conditions.
The Jackhammer is a fully automatic, drum-fed shotgun capable of unleashing a torrent of shells in seconds. Rare and highly prized, it is a force to be reckoned with in any firefight.
A classic lever-action shotgun, prized for its fast cycling and reliability. Popular among ranchers and lawmen, it delivers solid stopping power and can be fired rapidly in skilled hands.

The Light Machine Gun is a pre-war, fully automatic support weapon chambered for 5.56mm rounds. Designed for sustained suppressive fire, it features a high-capacity magazine and a high rate of fire, making it ideal for pinning down enemies and providing covering fire in open combat.

Lincoln's Repeater is a legendary lever-action rifle, renowned for its exceptional craftsmanship and historical significance. With its polished finish and unmatched accuracy, this weapon stands as a symbol of freedom and precision, making it a prized possession among collectors and marksmen alike.

Lucky is a legendary .357 Magnum revolver, distinguished by its immaculate chrome finish and ornate engravings. Renowned for its flawless action and uncanny accuracy, this weapon is favored by gunslingers who trust in both skill and fortune to carry the day.

Maria is a beautifully engraved 9mm pistol, adorned with intricate silver inlays and a pearl grip. Once belonging to a figure of legend, it combines elegance with deadly precision, making it a prized weapon for those who value both style and performance.
A lightweight, semi-automatic carbine chambered for 5.56mm rounds. Designed for precision and medium-range engagements, it was favored by NCR marksmen for its accuracy and reliability.

The Minigun is a multi-barrel, rotary machine gun capable of firing 5mm rounds at an extremely high rate. Its devastating firepower makes it a favorite for heavy support roles, but its weight and bulk limit mobility.
An early model Avenger heavy weapon, known for its high rate of fire and reliability.

The MkII Avenger is a legendary minigun chambered in 5mm, renowned for its devastating rate of fire and rugged reliability. Favored by elite operatives, its advanced design and custom modifications make it one of the most formidable automatic weapons in the wasteland.

Ol' Painless is a legendary hunting rifle, renowned for its exceptional accuracy and lightweight design. Passed down through generations, this rifle combines reliability with precision, making it a prized weapon for any marksman.

A crude, homemade bolt-action rifle assembled from scavenged pipes and scrap metal. While slow to fire and lacking in power compared to proper rifles, it is easy to produce and maintain, making it a common weapon among wasteland survivors.

A crude, homemade pistol cobbled together from scavenged pipes and scrap metal. While unreliable and lacking in stopping power, it is easy to craft and maintain, making it a common sidearm among the most desperate wastelanders.
A crude, homemade revolver assembled from scavenged pipes and scrap metal. While unreliable and slow to reload, it is easy to craft and maintain, making it a common sidearm among wasteland survivors.

A crude, homemade semi-automatic rifle assembled from scavenged pipes and scrap metal. While unreliable and inaccurate, it offers decent firepower for its simple construction, making it popular among raiders and settlers alike.
A crude, single-shot shotgun cobbled together from pipes and scrap. Devastating at close range but slow to reload, it's a favorite among desperate wastelanders who need stopping power on a budget.
A crude, long-barreled pipe rifle fitted with a makeshift scope. While nowhere near as accurate as a proper sniper rifle, it offers improved range for wastelanders who can't afford better equipment.

The Assault Rifle is a pre-war, fully automatic rifle chambered for 5.56mm rounds. Its distinctive drum magazine and robust construction make it a favorite for sustained fire in the wasteland, though it is heavy and unwieldy compared to more modern designs requiring the use of power armor.

The R-91 is a pre-war, fully automatic assault rifle chambered for 5.56mm rounds. It was the standard-issue rifle for the U.S. Armed Forces before the Great War, known for its reliability and versatility in combat.

Ratslayer is a unique, silenced varmint rifle equipped with a custom scope and lightweight frame. Renowned for its exceptional accuracy and stealth, it excels at eliminating small targets from a distance without drawing attention. Its precision engineering and quiet operation make it a favorite among marksmen and hunters alike.

Reservist's Rifle is a rare, finely-crafted sniper rifle once carried by an elite military marksman. Lightweight and exceptionally accurate, it is prized for its long-range precision and quick handling, making it a coveted weapon among sharpshooters and collectors alike.
This pre-War, semi-automatic combat shotgun was designed for law enforcement and military use. Its high rate of fire and large magazine make it devastating in close-quarters combat.
A double-barreled shotgun with the barrels and stock cut down for easier concealment. Extremely deadly at point-blank range, but wildly inaccurate and short-ranged.

The Scoped Hunting Rifle is a pre-war bolt-action rifle chambered for .308 rounds. Equipped with a scope, it is highly accurate and effective for medium to long-range engagements, making it a reliable choice for hunters and marksmen alike.

The Silenced 10mm Pistol is a semi-automatic sidearm chambered for 10mm rounds and fitted with an integral suppressor. Favored by assassins and covert operatives, it offers a balance of stopping power and stealth.

The Silenced .22 Pistol is a lightweight, semi-automatic sidearm chambered for .22 LR rounds and equipped with an integral suppressor. Extremely quiet and easy to control, it is favored for stealth operations but lacks stopping power.

A lightweight, fully automatic submachine gun chambered for .22 LR rounds and equipped with an integral suppressor. Extremely quiet and easy to control, but lacks stopping power compared to larger calibers.
A simple, single-barrel break-action shotgun. Cheap to produce and easy to maintain, but slow to reload and limited to one shot at a time.

The Trail Carbine is a lever-action rifle chambered for .44 Magnum rounds. Renowned for its ruggedness and accuracy, it is a favorite among wasteland scouts and hunters for its fast cycling and reliable stopping power.

Vance's 9mm SMG is a legendary submachine gun, expertly customized for rapid fire and superior handling. Its polished finish and unique engravings set it apart, while its enhanced accuracy and reliability make it a prized weapon among collectors and gunslingers alike.

The Varmint Rifle is a lightweight, bolt-action rifle chambered for .223 rounds. Originally designed for pest control and small game hunting, it is accurate and easy to handle, but lacks the stopping power of larger rifles.

The Victory Rifle is a legendary, custom sniper rifle renowned for its exceptional stopping power and unique knockdown capability. Its reinforced frame and precision engineering make it a prized weapon among sharpshooters, able to deliver devastating shots at extreme range while reliably staggering targets.
A powerful heavy weapon, designed for maximum suppression and area denial.

Wanda is a unique, custom combat shotgun from the Capital Wasteland. Distinguished by its green finish and reliability, it features a faster firing rate and lighter weight compared to standard combat shotguns. Favored by those who value speed and dependability in close-quarters combat.
A soft beret worn by Brotherhood detachments in Appalachia. Embroidered with the Brotherhood crest, it is used for identification and ceremonial duties by officers and specialized units.
Standard-issue Brotherhood of Steel fatigues. Made from a robust and durable material, these fatigues are worn by members of the Brotherhood during their daily operations in Appalachia.
Standard-issue hood used by Brotherhood infantry for concealment and basic protection from the elements during patrols and tactical operations.

A pristine military dress uniform worn by Brotherhood infantry during formal occasions. The uniform bears the insignia of the Brotherhood of Steel and represents the discipline and order of the organization.

Advanced T-60 power armor bearing the distinctive colors and insignia of the Appalachian Brotherhood of Steel. This battle-tested armor provides exceptional protection while maintaining mobility, representing the pinnacle of pre-war American military engineering adapted for wasteland operations.

Advanced T-60 power armor helmet featuring integrated HUD systems and environmental sensors. The distinctive Appalachian Brotherhood insignia marks this as belonging to the faction that emerged from Vault 76 to reclaim the wasteland and preserve humanity's technological heritage.
BoS Appalachia T-60 Power Armor Helmet

A vacuum-sealed asbestos jumpsuit covering the entire body. Its purpose is twofold: it allows the user to actually operate the armor and protect soft tissue from moving parts inside the suit and heat. Unlike the BOS uniform, the Recon Armor provides decent protection on its own and is often used by Brotherhood agents on scouting missions or other situations where the clunky and loud power armor would draw too much attention.
A reinforced combat helmet issued to Brotherhood ground troops. Built to resist shrapnel and small-arms fire, commonly paired with infantry armor on patrols and engagements.
A sturdy military coat worn by Brotherhood officers. The coat features the Brotherhood's insignia and is designed to withstand the rigors of combat while maintaining a professional appearance.
A leather flight jacket worn by Brotherhood pilots and Vertibird crew members. The jacket features the Brotherhood's gear and sword emblem and provides protection during flight operations.

Standard-issue field uniform of the Brotherhood of Steel. These durable fatigues are worn by enlisted personnel and junior officers during daily operations and provide basic protection against environmental hazards.

Specialized medical uniform worn by Brotherhood field medics. The fatigues feature additional pockets for medical supplies and are designed to resist contamination during treatment of wounded personnel.
Enhanced combat armor upgraded with Brotherhood of Steel technology. This armor retains the design of the original while incorporating improved protection and environmental sealing systems.
Ceremonial robes worn by Brotherhood Elders. These robes symbolize the authority and responsibility that comes with leading the Brotherhood of Steel in their sacred mission to preserve technology.
Field uniform worn by Brotherhood Scribes assigned to research missions outside the safety of Brotherhood facilities. The uniform combines scholarly tradition with practical field requirements.
Field hat worn by Brotherhood scribes and researchers on expeditions. It offers sun and light-weather protection while marking the wearer as part of the research and recovery cadre.
Pilot helmet used by Brotherhood aircrews and vertibird crews, fitted with padding and basic communications gear to protect crew during flight and rotor operations.

A jumpsuit designed from the ground up for use in conjunction with the West Tek power armor frame. It is a form-fitting suit designed for maximum comfort and utility while operating the armor. Although it is not a requirement for operating the armor, it makes long-term use more comfortable and reduces chafing.
A peaked cap worn by officers of lancer units to denote rank and authority within the Brotherhood during inspections and official duties.

A formal dress uniform worn by Lancer Captains of the Brotherhood of Steel. The uniform features gold braiding and ceremonial shoulder boards, marking the wearer as an officer of the Brotherhood's air fleet.

T-51 power armor in the classic colors of the original Brotherhood chapter from Lost Hills. This armor represents the foundation of Brotherhood traditions and the original mission to preserve and control pre-war technology for the betterment of humanity.
BoS Lost Hills T-51 Power Armor

The distinctive power armor of the Midwestern Brotherhood of Steel, featuring a unique horned helmet design that has become their signature on the battlefield. This armor represents the Midwest chapter's adaptive nature - maintaining Brotherhood standards while developing their own identity separate from Lost Hills. The intimidating silhouette strikes fear into raiders and mutants alike across the wastes of the former American Midwest.

The iconic horned helmet of the Midwestern Brotherhood of Steel. This distinctive headgear has become synonymous with the Midwest chapter's pragmatic but formidable approach to wasteland warfare. The intimidating horned design serves both as a symbol of their independence from Lost Hills and as a psychological weapon against their enemies.

Specialized reconnaissance armor worn by Brotherhood Outcast scouts and advance teams. This armor has been modified with enhanced mobility systems and stealth enhancements, featuring the distinctive Outcast markings and tactical modifications that reflect their more aggressive operational doctrine.

A tactical hood worn by Brotherhood Outcast reconnaissance specialists. This hood features enhanced optics integration, communication equipment, and environmental protection systems modified to Outcast specifications for extended field operations behind enemy lines.

Functional robes worn by Brotherhood Outcast specialists and technical personnel. These robes feature the distinctive Outcast coloring and insignia, modified for field operations where scholars and engineers work alongside warriors in the pursuit of technological supremacy.

Lightweight tactical armor used by Brotherhood reconnaissance units. The Recon Armor provides solid protection while maintaining mobility and stealth capabilities, making it ideal for scouting missions and situations where full power armor would be impractical. Features advanced composite materials and integrated sensor systems.
Lightweight hood favored by recon teams. Woven for low visibility and reduced glare to aid scouting, observation, and stealthy movement.
Laboratory attire worn by Science Scribes of the Brotherhood of Steel. The clean, white coat is designed for work with dangerous pre-war technology and experimental equipment.
Cap worn by science scribes to identify members of the Brotherhood's research corps. Scribes focus on study, salvage, and the preservation of prewar technology.
Hooded robes worn by Brotherhood Scribes during formal ceremonies. These robes represent the scholarly traditions of the Scribe order and their dedication to preserving technological knowledge.
Training uniform worn by Brotherhood Squires. This practical outfit is designed for combat training and education while marking the wearer's junior status within the Brotherhood hierarchy.
A simple cap issued to squires and trainees. Squires are apprentices attached to knights and paladins, learning fieldcraft and Brotherhood traditions.

T-45 power armor bearing the distinctive markings of Elder Lyons' progressive Brotherhood. This armor represents a departure from traditional Brotherhood doctrine, used by soldiers who prioritized protecting the innocent over hoarding technology, marking a controversial but heroic chapter in Brotherhood history.

T-45 power armor helmet worn by soldiers loyal to Elder Lyons' humanitarian mission. This helmet symbolizes a Brotherhood more concerned with protecting the innocent than hoarding technology, representing hope and compassion in the wasteland.
BoS Lyons T-45 Power Armor Helmet

T-45 power armor worn by Brotherhood Outcasts, painted in their characteristic black and red colors. These hardline traditionalists broke away from Elder Lyons' compassionate policies, believing the Brotherhood should focus solely on acquiring technology rather than helping wastelanders.

T-45 power armor helmet painted in the black and red colors of the Brotherhood Outcasts. This helmet represents unwavering dedication to the traditional Brotherhood mission of technology acquisition above all else, worn by those who refused to compromise their principles.
BoS Outcast T-45 Power Armor Helmet

T-51 power armor customized for the harsh Appalachian environment. This variant features enhanced environmental sealing and radiation shielding, adapted for the unique challenges faced by the Brotherhood's Appalachian chapter in their mission to reclaim and protect technology.

T-51 power armor helmet modified for the unique challenges of Appalachian operations. Enhanced with improved radiation detection systems and environmental filtering, essential for surviving the irradiated mountains and forests of post-war Appalachia.
BoS Appalachia T-51  Power Armor Helmet

The venerable T-51 power armor, a mainstay of Brotherhood forces. While older than the T-60 series, this armor has proven its reliability through decades of wasteland combat. Its tried-and-true design offers excellent protection and has been extensively field-tested by Brotherhood knights and paladins.

Time-tested T-51 power armor helmet that has protected Brotherhood soldiers for generations. While lacking some of the advanced features of newer models, its proven reliability and rugged construction make it a trusted companion in the wasteland.

Standard issue T-60 power armor worn by Brotherhood of Steel soldiers. Built for maximum protection and battlefield durability, this armor represents the apex of pre-war power armor technology, enhanced with post-war modifications and field improvements developed by Brotherhood engineers.

Standard issue T-60 power armor helmet worn by Brotherhood knights and paladins. Features advanced targeting systems, environmental protection, and the iconic red optical sensors that strike fear into the hearts of those who would misuse technology.

An official paper currency issued by the Brotherhood of Steel. These notes are backed by the Brotherhood's vast technological resources and can be exchanged for advanced equipment, supplies, and services within Brotherhood controlled territories. More valuable than standard bottle caps due to the organization's strict resource management and reputation for honoring debts.

A precision-engineered 10mm pistol manufactured by the Brotherhood of Steel. The Crusader represents the Brotherhood's philosophy of combining proven ballistic technology with modern manufacturing techniques. Featuring a reinforced frame, enhanced rifling, and superior materials, this sidearm offers exceptional reliability and accuracy compared to standard wasteland pistols. Brotherhood knights and paladins carry these as secondary weapons, trusting in their consistent performance when every shot counts.

A refined plasma rifle developed and manufactured by the Brotherhood of Steel's West Coast chapters. The M-94 represents decades of Brotherhood weapons research, featuring improved magnetic confinement, enhanced cooling systems, and superior durability compared to standard plasma rifles. Each weapon is carefully crafted by Brotherhood scribes and tested to rigorous military standards. The Brotherhood's trademark attention to detail and technological excellence make this one of the most reliable plasma weapons in the wasteland.

A cutting-edge energy weapon developed by the Brotherhood of Steel using ultracite, a rare crystalline material with exceptional energy-conducting properties. This experimental rifle fires rapid bursts of ultracite-charged plasma that penetrates armor more effectively than standard plasma weapons while maintaining a higher rate of fire. The weapon's advanced cooling system and compact design make it a favorite among Brotherhood field operatives who need to engage heavily armored targets quickly. Each rifle is painstakingly assembled in Brotherhood workshops from carefully refined ultracite cores.

A Brotherhood of Steel ration crate containing preserved military meals, purified water, and nutritional supplements. Designed to sustain soldiers during extended operations in the harsh wasteland environment.

A specialized Brotherhood of Steel uniform crate containing the distinctive gear worn by Elder Lyons' reformed Brotherhood. These uniforms represent the faction's humanitarian mission and their commitment to protecting the innocent.

A Brotherhood Outcast supply crate containing the distinctive red and black uniforms worn by the purist faction. These uniforms symbolize their unwavering dedication to the original Codex and their mission to reclaim pre-war technology at any cost.

A sealed Brotherhood of Steel supply crate containing essential technological components, spare parts, and advanced materials. These resources are crucial for maintaining and upgrading their sophisticated equipment and installations.

A Brotherhood of Steel supply crate containing standard-issue uniforms, fatigues, and protective gear worn by regular Brotherhood personnel. Each piece bears the distinctive colors and insignia of the faction.

A sealed Brotherhood of Steel weapon crate containing high-quality firearms and energy weapons used by their forces. The distinctive red and metallic finish bears the Brotherhood's emblematic cogwheel.

A Brotherhood of Steel manufactured super sledgehammer, built to their exacting specifications. This legendary weapon combines a reinforced durasteel head with advanced kinetic energy storage technology. Upon impact, the stored energy releases in a devastating shockwave, multiplying the force of each blow. Brotherhood paladins favor this weapon for breaching fortifications and engaging heavily armored targets in close combat. The BoS insignia stamped on the handle marks this as official Brotherhood equipment, built to last through countless battles.

Some dogs have been domesticated, even charged with guarding its owner and all their property this animal will do so to the best of its ability. This animal has been bred by the legion as a scout and guard dog to counter any foes that may come its way.

The ornate ceremonial robes worn by Caesar himself, the supreme leader of the Legion. These magnificent red and gold robes are crafted from the finest pre-war materials and adorned with golden trim, symbolizing absolute authority over the Legion. The robes provide modest protection but their true power lies in the awe and respect they command from all who see them.

A heavy suit of armor worn by the Legionï¿½s centurions, the elite of the Legion. Itï¿½s composed of a wild assortment of different armors, including pieces of combat armor, power armor leather armor and even super mutant armor. These pieces are likely taken from enemies defeated by the wearer. Despite being such a crude assortment of different armors, it offers superior protection.
Worn by the advance scouts of Caesar's Legion, the vexillarii, this helmet is fashioned from the pelt from the head of a coyote. It offers little protection as its main use is symbolic in nature.

A lightweight suit of armor worn by Legion Scouts, very similar in appearance to the recruit armor. Like most Legion armor, it is made from repurposed sports equipment, consisting of a football player's protective shoulder and chest pads reinforced with additional leather padding and worn over a baseball catcher's vest. It doesnï¿½t protect very well.

A basic, lightweight helmet composed of a thick brown cloth headwrap featuring a visor to shield the wearerï¿½s eyes from the scorching sun. Worn by the explorers, scouts in service of Caesarï¿½s Legion

The armor of a Legate, a close confidante of the mighty Caesar himself. A full suit of heavy gauge steel, bearing numerous scratches and dents from countless battles, and offers full body protection. It was crafted to closely resemble the armors of the ancient romans and has little to do with the repurposed sports equipment most members of the Legion wear. Unsurprisingly it provides itï¿½s wearer with superior protection but is incredibly heavy.

The helmet of a legate, the highest-ranking warlords in the Legion, apart from Caesar of course. Its fashioned after the enclosed masks often worn by the cavalry of ancient Rome and sports red frills and horns to make the wearer look more intimidating.
A set of Legion plate armor, usauly made of scavenged pre-war football pads with nails added to the shoulders for extra protection.

This armor commonly worn by Caesarï¿½s personal guard is similar to the recruit armor, but sports additional metal bracers on the forearms, probably because of the Praetorianï¿½s preference for punching people with their fists, as well as a short red cape and a red cowl. Despite its close resemblance, this armor protects itï¿½s wearer a lot better than the recruit armor while weighing about the same.

The Legionï¿½s main fighting force, the prime legionaries have earned their right to wear better armor compared to the recruits. Itï¿½s still made with the same protective shoulder and chest pads but features additional armor bands wrapped around the upper body.

A pre-war football helmet adorned with feathers, worn with a red face wrap and black goggles. While it offers a decent amount of protection, its main use is to serve as a symbol of rank. The black feathers fastened on the helmet identify the wearer as a prime decanus, a middle rank of officer within Caesarï¿½s Legion, leading a squad of prime legionaries.

The prime helmet is a brown cap that is equipped with black protective goggles and a red bandanna is wrapped around the lower face. Itï¿½s essentially the same helmet as the recruitsï¿½ one but features a different coloured bandana to mark the different ranks.

Like most Legion armor, the recruit armor is made from repurposed sports equipment, consisting of a football player's protective shoulder and chest pads reinforced with additional leather padding and worn over a baseball catcher's vest.

A pre-war football helmet adorned with feathers, worn with a red face wrap and black goggles. While it offers a decent amount of protection, its main use is to serve as a symbol of rank. The red and black feathers fastened on the helmet identify the wearer as a recruit decanus, the most junior rank of officer within Caesarï¿½s Legion, leading a squad of inexperienced recruits.
A brown leather cap worn with black protective goggles and the lower face is wrapped with a bandana. Not the best helmet, but it is easy to make and lightweight.
The common tunic worn by the members of Caesarï¿½s Legion.

A lightweight leather vest used by the Legion. The base of the vest seams to be made from an old baseball umpires pads, or even some football training pads. Either way it gives the user some more protection than nothing at all.

Compared to regular legionariesï¿½ armor, veteran armor has additional armor pads on the lower legs, as well as several sharp nails sticking out of the shoulder pads and the leather wrappings on the right arm. It offers more protection while still being somewhat light.

A pre-war football helmet adorned with feathers, worn with a red face wrap and black goggles. While it offers a decent amount of protection, its main use is to serve as a symbol of rank. The red, white and black feathers fastened on the helmet identify the wearer as a veteran decanus, a higher-ranking officer within Caesarï¿½s Legion, leading a squad of hardened veteran legionaries.
A baseball cap with an aluminium helmet, goggles for the eyes, and a face wrap. It offers a bit more protection compared to the helmets of the Legionï¿½s lower ranks while not weighing more.

Based on a suit of Legion veteran armor, with the addition of circular metal plates attached to the torso, as well as a banner displaying the flag of the Legion worn on the back. While it is a step up from the lower ranksï¿½ armor, it is also considerably heavier.
Worn by the advance scouts of Caesar's Legion, the vexillarii, this helmet is fashioned from the pelt from the head of a coyote. It offers little protection as its main use is symbolic in nature.
Caesar's Legion uses this coin as one of the primary forms of currency. This coin bears an image of Caesar himself on one side and Caesar, Josua Grahm and Bill Calhoun on the other.

A powerful anti-addiction medication that purges the body of chemical dependencies. Addictol works by resetting neurological pathways and breaking the addiction cycle, though the process is uncomfortable and temporary exhaustion is common.\n\nA lifesaver for those trying to kick dangerous habits, though its high cost limits availability.

An automated delivery system that dispenses a standard stimpak dose when injuries are detected. The auto-injector mechanism eliminates the need for manual application, making it ideal for solo travelers.

An automated delivery system loaded with super stimpak formula for rapid emergency healing. This advanced injector combines military-grade healing compounds with automated delivery technology, making it one of the most valuable medical items in the wasteland.

A Mentats variant infused with berry flavoring and enhanced formula. Berry Mentats significantly boost charisma and social skills, making the user more persuasive and likable in negotiations and social situations.\n\nFavored by traders, diplomats, and anyone who needs to make a good impression.

A sterile bag of preserved blood suitable for emergency transfusions. Blood packs provide immediate restoration of blood volume and help stabilize critically wounded patients. While not as advanced as modern medical technology, these pre-war medical supplies remain valuable in the wasteland.\n\nBlood packs are universal donors and work with all blood types.

A pre-war performance-enhancing steroid. Buffout dramatically increases strength and endurance, improving melee damage and carrying capacity. However, prolonged use leads to addiction and potential organ damage.\n\nPopular with athletes and laborers before the war, now a staple among wasteland fighters and scavengers.

An experimental drug that temporarily enhances night vision and low-light perception. Cateye dilates the pupils and enhances retinal sensitivity, allowing users to see clearly in near-total darkness.\n\nValued by night scouts and scavengers exploring dark ruins, though the light sensitivity during daytime can be problematic.

A pre-war cognitive enhancer that boosts intelligence and perception at the cost of social skills. Daddy-O makes the user smarter and more perceptive but more socially awkward and withdrawn.\n\nFavored by researchers and technicians who prioritize mental acuity over social interaction.

A pre-war recreational drug that enhances mood and social charm. Day Tripper makes the user more charismatic and likable while providing a pleasant mild high. Moderately addictive with regular use.\n\nPopular in pre-war social circles, still used by those seeking to improve their social standing.
A watered-down stimulation delivery system that provides minor healing over time. Common among wasteland scavengers due to its lower cost and availability.

A refined and purified version of Jet created through an advanced filtration process. Dixon's Jet provides a stronger, longer-lasting high with fewer impurities than standard Jet, though it remains highly addictive.\n\nNamed after its creator, this premium variant commands high prices among Jet users.

A comprehensive medical kit containing surgical tools, antiseptics, splints, and pharmaceutical supplies. Doctor's bags allow field treatment of serious injuries and crippled limbs, though proper application requires medical knowledge and time.\n\nAn essential piece of equipment for any serious wasteland medic.

A specialized anti-addiction compound that helps break chemical dependencies. While less powerful than Addictol, Fixer is more readily available and still effective at combating withdrawal symptoms and reducing addiction severity.\n\nA wasteland doctor's tool for helping addicts recover without access to expensive pre-war medications.

A balanced Mentats variant that enhances both intelligence and charisma. Grape Mentats make the user both smarter and more socially adept, excellent for technical negotiations or scientific presentations.\n\nThe choice of scientists who need to explain complex ideas to non-experts.

A traditional wasteland medicine made from ground broc flower and xander root. While less effective than modern stimpaks, healing powder is cheaper to produce and has been used by tribal healers for generations. The mixture provides gradual healing over time.

An experimental pre-war drug designed to rapidly heal crippled limbs. Hydra uses advanced regenerative compounds to repair damaged tissue and bone, though the process is painful and can cause temporary disorientation.\n\nPopular among wasteland combatants for its ability to get wounded soldiers back into the fight quickly.

A highly addictive meta-amphetamine created from brahmin dung fumes. Jet provides a significant boost to action speed and reflexes, but crashes hard and is notoriously difficult to quit.\n\nInvented by a chem genius named Myron, Jet became the most widespread and devastating drug in the post-war wasteland.

A pre-war pain suppression medication. Med-X works by blocking pain receptors and reducing incoming damage through enhanced pain tolerance. The drug is highly effective but carries significant risk of addiction.\n\nOriginally marketed as a general anesthetic, Med-X has become a valuable combat drug in the wasteland.

A nootropic drug that enhances cognitive function. Mentats improve perception, intelligence, and mental acuity, making users sharper and more aware of their surroundings. Prolonged use can lead to psychological dependency.\n\nFavored by scientists, traders, and anyone needing a mental edge in negotiations or technical work.

A specialized Mentats variant that dramatically enhances perception and awareness. Orange Mentats sharpen the senses and improve accuracy, making them invaluable for scouts, snipers, and anyone needing enhanced sensory perception.\n\nA marksman's secret weapon for those critical shots.

A recreational Mentats variant designed for social gatherings. Party Time Mentats boost charisma and mood, making social interactions more enjoyable and successful while providing a mild euphoric effect.\n\nThe life of any wasteland party, though best used sparingly.

A military combat drug that sends the user into a berserker rage. Psycho dramatically increases damage output and resistance to injury, but suppresses higher brain functions and is highly addictive.\n\nDeveloped before the war for special forces operations, Psycho turns ordinary soldiers into fearless killing machines.

A pre-war intravenous chemical solution that bonds with radiation contamination in the body and flushes it out through the bloodstream. While effective at treating toxic buildup, the process is exhausting and leaves the patient weakened for several hours.\n\nRad-Away is a valuable commodity in the wasteland, where radiation exposure is a constant threat.

A pre-war anti-radiation chemical that significantly increases the body's resistance to toxic and radioactive contamination. Rad-X works by temporarily altering cellular structure to resist radiation absorption, though this process causes minor disorientation and reduced reflexes.\n\nUnlike Rad-Away, which removes existing contamination, Rad-X is taken preventatively before exposure to hazardous environments.

A basic anti-addiction medication that helps reduce withdrawal symptoms and cravings. While not as powerful as Addictol, Rebound is more affordable and can still provide significant relief for those struggling with chemical dependencies.\n\nA common choice for wasteland clinics treating addicted patients on a budget.

A dangerous mixture of Jet and Nuka-Cola that provides an intense burst of energy and speed. The combination of stimulants creates a powerful but unstable high that significantly boosts action speed and reflexes.\n\nPopular among couriers and raiders who need quick bursts of performance.

A brutal combat stimulant specifically designed to enhance melee combat effectiveness. Slasher increases melee damage output dramatically while reducing pain sensitivity, creating fearless close-combat fighters.\n\nPopular among raiders and melee specialists, though its addictive nature and side effects make it a dangerous choice.

A muscle relaxant and neural stabilizer that dramatically improves shooting accuracy and reduces weapon sway. Steady calms tremors and steadies the hands, making it invaluable for precision work and sniping.\n\nDeveloped for marksmen and surgeons, Steady has become essential for anyone requiring perfect hand-eye coordination.

A standard stimulation delivery system for healing injuries. The most common medical item in the wasteland, stimpaks use a combination of healing agents and bio-regenerative compounds to rapidly mend wounds.

A potent military-grade stimulation pack that provides rapid and extensive healing. Super stimpaks use a highly concentrated formula with advanced bio-regenerative compounds, making them extremely valuable and rare.

An extremely potent inhaler that dramatically accelerates the user's perception and reflexes. Turbo makes time seem to slow down, allowing for superhuman reaction speeds. The effect is short-lived but incredibly powerful.\n\nCreated through an advanced refinement process, Turbo is one of the most sought-after combat enhancers in the wasteland.
An enhanced stimulation pack with improved healing properties and faster action. These improved stimpaks use a more concentrated formula with additional bio-regenerative compounds.

An extremely potent combination of Jet and Psycho. Ultrajet provides the speed enhancement of Jet combined with the combat fury of Psycho, creating a devastating combat stimulant. Highly addictive and dangerous.\n\nA favorite among the most desperate and violent wasteland fighters.

An extremely rare and expensive pre-war super-drug that enhances all physical and mental capabilities simultaneously. X-Cell provides comprehensive enhancement across all stats but is incredibly difficult and costly to produce.\n\nA pinnacle of pre-war pharmaceutical achievement, X-Cell is the ultimate performance enhancer.

A suit of advanced power armor developed by the Enclave. Offers exceptional protection against all forms of damage, enhanced mobility, and complete environmental sealing. Wearing this armor marks its user as a force to be reckoned with on the battlefield.
An advanced helmet designed for Enclave soldiers, offering exceptional protection against ballistic, blunt, and heat damage. Equipped with integrated systems for toxic environment resistance.
The MkII variant of the Enclave Advanced Power Armor, featuring improved environmental sealing and enhanced durability for prolonged combat scenarios.
The Mk II variant of the Advanced Power Armor Helmet, featuring enhanced durability and improved environmental resistance for hazardous conditions.
Enclave Advanced Power Armor Mk II Helmet

Advanced combat armor issued to Enclave troopers. Constructed from reinforced composites, it offers substantial protection against ballistic and melee threats while allowing for mobility in hostile environments.

The Hellfire Power Armor is an advanced Enclave suit designed for extreme environments and frontline assault. Its reinforced plating and advanced heat dispersal systems provide unmatched protection against fire and explosions, making its wearer a walking fortress in the most hazardous conditions.
A heavy-duty helmet for the Hellfire Power Armor, offering unparalleled protection and resistance to extreme environmental conditions.
A distinguished cap worn by Enclave officers, symbolizing authority and discipline. Made from high-quality materials, it offers moderate protection and a professional appearance.

A formal coat worn by Enclave officers, tailored from high-quality materials and adorned with insignia. It signifies authority and provides moderate protection while maintaining a commanding presence.
Specialized boots worn by Enclave scientists, designed with reinforced soles and advanced materials to protect against chemical spills, radiation, and hazardous terrain during field research.
Protective gloves worn by Enclave scientists, made from advanced polymers to shield hands from chemicals, contaminants, and the harsh elements encountered during fieldwork.
An advanced helmet designed for Enclave scientists, equipped with protective visors and integrated sensors to assist in hazardous environments.

A suit commonly worn by the Enclave's scientists, especially when working in the hazardous wasteland. It's a fully enclosed white suit with special polymers woven into it and a helmet with an orange visor made from plexiglass. It comes with a built-in computer system and various scientific instruments to support all manners of intellectual pursuits.

Advanced combat armor issued to Enclave troopers. Constructed from reinforced composites, it offers substantial protection against ballistic and melee threats while allowing for mobility in hostile environments.
A practical cap issued to Enclave troopers, providing basic protection and shielding against the elements while maintaining a professional look.

A jet-black uniform with a tan jacket and pants that are worn over it. The outer layer is made of a sturdy material designed to withstand the harsh conditions of the wasteland. The uniform comes with a pair of knee-high boots, black gloves, and a utility belt. It does protect well against the elements, less so against bullets.
A next-generation Enclave power armor, the X-02 offers formidable protection and advanced environmental sealing. It is lighter and more intimidating than earlier models.
The standard helmet for the X02 Power Armor, providing balanced protection and advanced features for Enclave operatives in the field.

A rare variant of the X-02, the Tesla Power Armor is fitted with electromagnetic shielding and energy-dispersing coils. It offers superior protection against heat and electrical attacks, making its wearer a terrifying sight on the battlefield.
A specialized helmet for the X02 Tesla Power Armor, designed to protect against energy-based attacks while maintaining high resistance to environmental hazards.
Enclave X02 Tesla Power Armor Helmet

High-purity platinum coins minted by the Enclave as a reserve currency for high-value transactions. Each bullion coin bears the distinctive Enclave eagle emblem and represents the technological supremacy and economic power of America's shadow government. These precious metal coins are highly valued both for their platinum content and as symbols of Enclave authority.

Vertibird is a designation for a series of vertical take-off and landing (VTOL) aircraft. These multipurpose tiltrotor aircraft were developed by the United States military and rapidly became a primary gunship and transport aircraft. This one is now in use by the Enclave.

An Enclave refinement of the multiplas design, featuring three independently-controlled plasma generation chambers in a triangular array. Each trigger pull unleashes a devastating barrage of three plasma bolts simultaneously. The advanced targeting computer helps maintain accuracy despite the weapon's aggressive spread pattern, making it a feared close-to-medium range weapon.

The pinnacle of Enclave plasma rifle technology. This advanced assault variant features an upgraded plasma generation chamber, enhanced magnetic confinement, and superior targeting systems. Significantly more accurate and reliable than standard plasma rifles, it represents the cutting edge of pre-war American military technology.

A devastating heavy weapon platform developed by the Enclave for anti-armor and siege operations. This massive plasma cannon generates and magnetically accelerates supercharged plasma bolts with enough energy to melt through bunker walls and power armor alike. The weapon's immense power comes at the cost of weight, slow cycling time, and the need for enhanced cooling systems. Typically mounted on vehicles or power armor, it can be wielded by sufficiently strong soldiers in desperate situations.

A compact plasma submachine gun developed by the Enclave for close-quarters operations. This weapon sacrifices range and raw power for a dramatically increased rate of fire, making it ideal for room clearing and urban combat. The miniaturized plasma generation system allows for rapid cycling between shots.

The Self-Modulating Energy Loadout Efficiency (SMELE) plasma rifle represents the Enclave's answer to the need for versatile energy weapons. This sophisticated platform features adaptive power regulation, allowing it to balance between sustained fire and devastating single shots. Its modular design incorporates both offensive and defensive systems, making it the preferred weapon of elite Enclave special operations units.
A Vault-Tec collectors edition lunchbox. It's a little dirty, but still opens and may have something useful inside!

An experimental variant of the AER laser rifle series, the AER14 prototype features improved focusing crystals and an advanced energy regulator. It delivers greater accuracy and damage than standard laser rifles, though its prototype status makes it rare and expensive to produce.

The Flamer Mk I is an early-generation incendiary weapon that sprays a stream of burning fuel at close to medium range. Favored by pre-war military forces for clearing trenches and fortifications, it excels at dealing with groups of enemies in confined spaces. Its crude but effective design makes it a terror on the battlefield.

An improved version of the original flamer design, the Mk II features enhanced fuel efficiency, greater range, and more consistent flame projection. Popular among pre-war assault teams and post-war scavengers alike, it represents the pinnacle of portable incendiary weapon technology before the war.

A huge and heavy laser weapon reminiscent of Minigun and popular with the Brotherhood of Steel. Its individual shots do little damage, but this is entirely compensated by its extremely high rate of fire. It excels at the same areas as the Minigun, providing suppressing fire over short and medium distances.

An early model of the Gatling Laser, this heavy weapon features a rotary barrel design that allows for sustained automatic fire. While less refined than later models, it remains a formidable suppressive weapon capable of laying down withering laser fire.

A devastating heavy weapon combining electromagnetic acceleration technology with minigun-style rotating barrels. The Gauss minigun fires a continuous stream of electromagnetically propelled projectiles capable of shredding armor and flesh alike. Its immense firepower comes at the cost of considerable weight and power requirements.

A masterwork gauss weapon designed for extreme long-range precision. This unique rifle features an extended barrel, advanced targeting optics, and refined electromagnetic coils that deliver unparalleled accuracy at distances that would make conventional snipers envious. Each shot represents the perfect marriage of pre-War science and deadly efficiency.

A pre-War German-designed plasma pistol that represents the pinnacle of compact energy weapon engineering. The Glock 86 features revolutionary magnetic field stabilization that dramatically improves accuracy and reduces cooldown time compared to standard plasma pistols. This exceptionally rare weapon was never mass-produced.

The Heavy Incinerator is a unique and devastating pre-war heavy flamethrower issued to elite Enclave Hellfire units. Built with reinforced components and an advanced fuel delivery system, it projects an intense stream of fire over considerable distances. This weapon is both a symbol of terror and a masterpiece of destructive engineering, capable of reducing enemies to ash.

A compact, shortened version of the standard laser rifle designed for close-quarters combat and urban operations. The laser carbine sacrifices some range and power for improved maneuverability and faster target acquisition, making it popular among scouts and special forces.

The AEP7 laser pistol comes with a generous battery size, tight shot grouping at range, resilience to extended use, and fairly decent damage output, it is a very good choice for a sidearm and is used extensively in this role by the Brotherhood of Steel and the Enclave. Like other energy weapons, the laser pistol has the ability to reduce enemies to piles of steaming ash.

The laser rapid capacitor weapon is a strange gun that mimics a submachinegun. The unique feature of the RCW is the addition of a drum magazine, which gives this weapon its unique look, high rate of fire, and name. This weapon exhibits moderate accuracy due to a high spread for a laser weapon, making it useful for taking down enemies at almost any range.

A staple of the pre-war armed forces, the laser rifle has become a somewhat unusual sight in the wasteland, especially one in good condition. They emit a concentrated beam of coherent light capable of severely damaging what- or whoever it was aimed at.

An experimental prototype of the M72 Gauss rifle featuring advanced magnetic coil configuration and enhanced power cell efficiency. The M72 A2 represents the pinnacle of pre-War electromagnetic weapon technology, with improved accuracy and greater armor penetration than its standard counterpart. Only a handful were ever produced before the Great War.

The M72 Gauss rifle is a top-of-the-line pre-War weapon utilizing electromagnetic coils to accelerate metallic projectiles to hypersonic velocities. Capable of punching through most armor with ease, the Gauss rifle is a testament to pre-War military innovation. Its precision and stopping power are legendary among wasteland survivors.

An advanced experimental weapon featuring a revolutionary hyperbreeder energy cell that regenerates ammunition at an unprecedented rate. This unique pistol can sustain continuous fire far longer than conventional energy weapons, though each shot is relatively weak. Its futuristic design and exceptional reliability make it a prized possession.

The Multiplas Rifle is a heavily modified plasma weapon that fires three plasma projectiles in a spread with each shot. While devastating at close range, its accuracy drops off quickly at distance, making it best suited for short- to mid-range combat.

An experimental plasma rifle prototype featuring an enhanced magnetic field generator and advanced plasma containment system. The P-94 fires more powerful plasma bolts with greater stability than standard plasma rifles. This unique weapon never progressed beyond field testing due to its complex maintenance requirements.

An unusually styled energy pistol with distinctive blue coloring. Despite its toy-like name and appearance, the Pew Pew delivers surprisingly powerful shots using an advanced energy focusing system. Its unique design makes it a conversation piece as much as a weapon.

The plasma caster functions very similar to its rifle-based counterpart. 
            It was first developed as an industrial tool but was soon after downscaled 
            and adapted as a weapon, itï¿½s still very heavy though. Compared to the plasma 
            rifle, it fires both faster and harder-hitting, but is significantly less accurate.

An earlier prototype of the Plasma Caster series. While lacking the refinement of later models, the Mk I is still a formidable heavy plasma weapon capable of delivering sustained plasma bombardment. Its crude magnetic confinement system makes it slightly less accurate but no less devastating.

A compact, refined plasma pistol designed for reliability and ease of maintenance. The Plasma Defender features improved magnetic bottle containment and more efficient energy conversion than standard plasma pistols, making it a favored sidearm among wastelanders who can maintain it.

The Plasma Machine Gun (PMG) is an experimental automatic plasma weapon that combines the rapid-fire capability of conventional machine guns with the devastating power of plasma technology. Using a revolutionary multi-cathode cyclic firing system, it can sustain fully automatic plasma fire at the cost of significant weight and power consumption. This unique heavy weapon was abandoned before mass production due to its extreme complexity.

A downscaled version of the plasma rifle. The plasma pistol has five tributary
            energy arcs connected to a central cathode, and the cathode functions as a point of
            focus, or the barrel. The energy is projected forward and forms a plasma pinch; though
            rather slow, the pinch carries high levels of both thermal and kinetic energy,
            inflicting massive damage.

The plasma rifle is a powerful energy weapon that fires superheated bolts of plasma. Favored by advanced factions, it delivers devastating damage and is effective against both armored and unarmored targets, though its projectiles travel slower than lasers.

The PPK12 Gauss pistol is a German-designed electromagnetic weapon that uses magnetic induction to propel projectiles at extremely high velocities. Compact and reliable, it delivers devastating kinetic energy with minimal recoil, making it an excellent sidearm for those who can afford such advanced technology.

An incredibly rare prototype plasma weapon developed by REPCONN Aerospace before the Great War. The Q-35 uses experimental matter-energy conversion technology to create exceptionally powerful plasma projectiles with reduced energy consumption. Its distinctive crystalline energy chamber and superior performance make it one of the most sought-after energy weapons in the wasteland.

The recharger pistol is a compact version of the recharger rifle, featuring a microfusion breeder that replenishes its ammunition automatically, no reloading required. While it can fire rapidly, the projectiles do less damage compared to a laser pistol. The breeder generates ammunition at a slow rate, meaning it can shoot bursts of projectiles fast, but there is considerable time in between those bursts. It is generally cheaper and more portable than the recharger rifle.

The recharger rifle was the first energy weapon featuring a microfusion breeder. It replenishes its ammunition automatically, no reloading required. However, the underlying technology wasnï¿½t very sophisticated yet. It can fire rapidly, but the projectiles do significantly less damage compared to a laser rifle. The inefficient breeder generates ammunition at a slow rate, meaning it can shoot bursts of projectiles fast, but there is a considerably time in between those bursts. At least it usually is a lot cheaper than a laser rifle.

A civilian laser pistol manufactured by Sprtel-Wood, the 9700 model was marketed as a home defense weapon before the war. While not as powerful as military-grade energy weapons, its reliability and ease of use made it popular among settlers and traders.

The tri-beam laser rifle appears to be a shortened laser rifle with a different emitter at its tip. It fires three beams of laser at once, serving as a high-tech alternative to shotguns. Like less advanced shotguns, the tri-beam laser rifle is best suited for combat over short ranges.

An older model laser pistol manufactured by Wattz Electronics before the Great War. The Wattz 1000 uses a simpler focusing lens system than modern designs, resulting in somewhat reduced performance, but its robust construction ensures reliable operation even decades after manufacture.

The rifle variant of Wattz Electronics' laser weapon line, the 2000 model features an extended barrel and improved power cell. While using older technology than modern AER series rifles, the Wattz 2000 remains a capable weapon favored for its durability and ease of maintenance.

The YK32 is a compact electromagnetic pulse weapon designed for close-quarters anti-mechanoid combat. It fires concentrated electromagnetic pulses that disrupt electronic systems and neural implants while dealing moderate kinetic damage. Particularly effective against mechanoids and shielded targets, though less lethal against unarmored organic targets than conventional weapons.

The YK42B represents the pinnacle of pre-War electromagnetic pulse weaponry. This military-grade pulse rifle fires high-intensity electromagnetic bursts capable of catastrophically disrupting mechanoid systems and neural networks at medium range. Its advanced capacitor array allows for sustained anti-mechanoid operations, making it invaluable in conflicts against automated forces. The weapon is particularly devastating against shielded and electronic targets, though its reduced lethality against unarmored organics limits its versatility.

Reinforced leather armor favored by Great Khans and other wasteland raiders. This armor combines flexibility with decent protection, featuring metal studs and plates sewn into heavy leather. It's ideal for those who value mobility and a menacing appearance.

A tribal helmet worn by Great Khans, decorated with war paint, feathers, and sometimes animal skulls. While it provides decent head protection, its primary purpose is to intimidate enemies and showcase the warrior's status within the tribe.

Lightweight armor worn by lower-ranking Great Khans. Made from scavenged materials and leather, it offers basic protection while maintaining mobility. The tribal designs mark the wearer as a member of the Great Khans.

Durable armor worn by Great Khan soldiers. Built from reinforced leather and scavenged metal plates, this armor provides substantial protection without sacrificing too much mobility. The skull emblems and war paint show this warrior has proven themselves in battle.

A stylish yet protective outfit worn by elite Great Khans and high-ranking members. This unique armor combines pre-war formal wear with strategically placed armor plating, creating a distinctive look that commands respect. It's a symbol of status within the tribe.
A pre-war golf club, the 9 iron. The weighted head and long shaft make for surprisingly effective swings. Fore!
A classic wooden baseball bat, repurposed as a simple but effective melee weapon. While not designed for combat, a solid swing can break bones and deter attackers.
A lightweight aluminum baseball bat. More durable than its wooden counterpart and capable of delivering faster, harder swings. A favorite among wasteland brawlers.
A baseball bat modified with circular saw blades attached to its head. A brutal and terrifying weapon that can slice through flesh and armor alike.
A baseball bat with nails and metal spikes driven through the head. Turns a simple blunt weapon into something far more vicious, capable of tearing flesh as well as breaking bones.
A simple wooden board with nails driven through it. Crude but effective, this makeshift weapon is common among raiders and desperate survivors.
A massive blade forged from a pre-war car bumper. Heavy and unwieldy, but capable of cleaving through enemies with tremendous force. A favorite of super mutants and other hulking wastelanders.
A pre-war chainsaw repurposed for combat. The roaring engine and spinning chain make for a terrifying and deadly weapon, capable of tearing through flesh and light armor.
A heavy butcher's cleaver designed for cutting through meat and bone. In the wasteland, it serves just as well against human targets.
A circular saw blade salvaged from a Mr. Handy robot, modified with a grip for handheld use. The spinning blade can slice through almost anything with ease.
A long wooden pool cue. While fragile compared to dedicated weapons, its reach and ability to deliver quick pokes make it useful in a pinch.
A heavy length of construction rebar wrapped with tape for a grip. Simple, brutal, and readily available in the ruins of the old world.
A compact handheld chainsaw designed by the U.S. military for close-quarters combat. The small, fast-spinning chain can rip through flesh and armor with horrifying efficiency.
A wooden rolling pin typically used for baking. In the wasteland, even kitchen utensils can become weapons. Light and fast, if not particularly deadly.
An improvised axe made from scrap metal and a wooden handle. Rough but effective for both chopping wood and splitting skulls.
A crude machete fashioned from scrap metal. The blade is rough but sharp enough to hack through flesh and vegetation alike.
A length of metal pipe scavenged from old plumbing or industrial ruins. Heavy and durable, making for a reliable bludgeoning weapon.

A legendary wasteland weapon - a makeshift flaming sword constructed from a motorcycle gas tank, pilot light, and lawnmower blade. The fuel-fed flames coat the blade, allowing it to slice and burn enemies simultaneously.
A heavy sledgehammer designed for demolition work. Slow to swing, but delivers devastating blows that can crush bone and dent armor.

A massive pre-war military sledgehammer enhanced with kinetic energy storage technology. Each swing releases tremendous force, capable of sending enemies flying and crushing power armor. A true wasteland legend.
A bandoleer often worn by NCR troopers. It's used to store ammunition as well as personal trinkets or talismans.
A simple mantle made from heavy fabrics. NCR officers often strap these to their shoulder. It doesn't offer much protection but insulates fairly well.
A large two headed bear from the mountains of California, this is the ideological and political symbol of the NCR and its military. This massive beast should be treated with respect and honor.

Some dogs have been domesticated, even charged with guarding its owner and all thier property this animal will do so to the best of its ability. While others have been specially bred to be a true battle buddy for the NCR. Tasked with defending troops from harm in the form of dangerous chems, explosives and enemy combatants themselves.
A stylish dress often worn by the ambassadors of the New California Republic. While it provides little to no actual\n            protection, it boosts its wearer's confidence during negotiation.
A stylish suit often worn by the ambassadors of the New California Republic. While it provides little to no actual protection, it boosts its wearer's confidence during negotiation.
A simple green beret usually worn by officers of the NCR army. It mainly serves as an insignia of rank but offers a fair bit of protection against the weather.
A red beret worn by the members of the famed 1st recon battalion. They are renowned as some of the best snipers the republic can muster. Fittingly, their motto is: â€œthe last thing you never see.â€

A khaki jumpsuit with many pockets and pouches attached. Itï¿½s commonly worn by engineers and other technical personnel associated with the NCR. Itï¿½s useful for carrying around a few spare tools but provides little protection in a firefight.
A trooper helmet with goggles and a piece of fabric attached to it. The fabric can be wrapped around the face to protect the wearer against the dust storms that are common in parts of the Mojave.
A trooper helmet with goggles and a piece of fabric attached to it. The fabric can be wrapped around the face to protect the wearer against the dust storms that are common in parts of the Mojave.
A cap adorned with the two-headed bear, the heraldic animal of the New California Republic. It serves as a symbol of rank for the republicâ€™s generals and is usually reserved for them.

A uniform reserved for the highest-ranking officers of the republicï¿½s army, the generals. Itï¿½s heavily decorated with motifs commonly associated with the NCR, such as the two-headed bear. It doesnï¿½t offer much protection, however, since the generals of the army rarely serve on the frontlines.
A cheap, mass-produced helmet worn by the NCRâ€™s soldiers. This version comes with a pair of goggles attached which provide additional, albeit modest, protection for the wearerâ€™s eyes.
A helmet with goggles usually worn by the members of the NCR military police. Like the uniform itself, it gives itâ€™s wearer an aura of authority.

A stylish suit often worn by the president of the New California Republic and similar high-ranking government officials. While it provides little to no actual protection, it gives its wearer an air of authority.

An outfit often worn by the rangers off-duty or underneath additional armour pieces. Itï¿½s reminiscent of the outfits worn by the cowboys of old. Itï¿½s quite rugged and offers good protection against the scorching sun but should not be the only protection a rangers has once the bullets start flying.
A brown cowboy hat favoured by the NCR rangers. Itâ€™s useful for protecting the wearer against the relentless heat of the wasteland.
A grey cowboy hat favoured by the NCR rangers. Itâ€™s useful for protecting the wearer against the relentless heat of the wasteland.
A simple cowboy hat favoured by the desert rangers, especially those patrolling the Mojave. Itâ€™s useful for protecting the wearer against the relentless heat of the wasteland.
A tan cowboy hat favoured by the NCR rangers. Itâ€™s useful for protecting the wearer against the relentless heat of the wasteland.

A suit of brownish combat armor, hand-manufactured in the NCR and most often worn by the NCR Rangers. As it is combat armor, itï¿½s sturdy and protects very well against damage, but weighs quite a lot.
Rugged clothes worn by the desert rangers. This particular outfit comes with a red scarf. Like most ranger clothes, it insulates well against the heat.
A rugged vest worn by NCR Rangers. It provides good protection against the elements and is ideal for long patrols and reconnaissance missions in harsh environments.
