A combat vest composed of the chestplate of a combat armour. Itï¿½s often worn by the rangers and offers formidable protection. Its biggest drawback is that it only covers the torso, leaving the appendages unprotected.

A duster often worn by the rangers. Itï¿½s main use is to protect the wearer against the sun and temperatures of the Mojave Desert, but itï¿½s thick enough to provide additional protection in a firefight as well.
A set of clothes made from tough materials such as leathers. Itï¿½s often worn underneath armour by the rangers and is particularly well insulating against heat.

A suit of T-45d power armor, captured by the NCR during the NCR-Brotherhood war. Technicians stripped out the servomotors and replaced the rad scrubbers with a custom air conditioning module, allowing it to be used by soldiers without the need of specialised training. This process, however, makes the armor much less effective. Despite that it still is, essentially, power armor and offers superior protection at the cost of enormous weight. It's usually only worn by the NCR heavy troopers.

A T-45 power armor helmet usually worn paired with the NCR's salvaged power armors. Despite its salvaged nature, it offers excellent protection and, in contrast to the armor itself, has no major drawbacks compared to a regular power armor.

An armor usually worn by the NCRï¿½s sniper squads and recon teams. Itï¿½s very similar looking to other NCR armor, but is darker for camouflage purposes. It provides good protection but, like most NCR armors, is quite heavy.

The standard armor for the rank and file of the NCR military forces. Itï¿½s composed of a khaki field jacket and breeches, an armored breastplate and brown combat boots. It protects its wearer well but is heavy.

This armor is essentially the regular NCR armed forces uniform composed of a khaki field jacket and breeches, an armored breastplate and brown combat boots, but features a bandoleer for carrying additional ammunition and other military equipment.

This light piece of clothing consists of the standard khaki field jacket and breeches but lacks the armored breastplate other NCR armors sport. As such it is lighter but offers only inferior protection.
The standard helmet of the NCR armed forces. It is mass-produced in the NCR and easy to make and maintain but offers only marginal protection.

This suit of armor is often worn by the sergeants and corporals of the NCR military. In addition to the standard khaki field jacket and breeches, as well as an armored breastplate, this armor also features a camouflaged cloth wrapped around the shoulder. It offers decent protection but is quite heavy.
A uniform labelling the wearer as a member of the NCR military police. Itï¿½s quite similar to other NCR uniforms but radiates the authority of the military police.

A vest often worn by NCR troopers. Itï¿½s composed of a reinforced chestplate and armoured pauldrons. While it offers quite a bit of additional protection, it only covers the torso and shoulders, leaving the lower body parts and the head unprotected.
An enclosed military helmet with built-in low light optics and a gas mask worn by NCR Rangers. It offers better protection in comparison to the Trooper helmet but is significantly rarer.
A riot helmet once worn by the guards of the NCRCF. Like many other parts of their gear, it has been apprehended by the Powder Gangers. It offers good protection, especially against blunt damage.
A padded vest often worn by the guards of the NCRCF. While less protective than full-blown armour, it guards its wearer quite well against small firearms.

A simple blue shirt with pants issued to the inmates of the NCRCF. These are simple clothes cheaply mass-produced in the NCR. As such they offer little to no protection in a fight but isolate quite well against the heat, since the NCR likes to use the inmates as roadworkers.

A simple overshirt worn by inmates of the NCRCF. Itï¿½s meant to identify the inmates as such and doesnï¿½t offer much protection against any kind of threat. Despite this, the Powder Gangers still wear these, probably because of the lack of alternative garments.

A combat vest composed of the chestplate of a combat armor. It�s often worn by the rangers and offers formidable protection. Its biggest drawback is that it only covers the torso, leaving the appendages unprotected. This set appears to have been customized with extra padding, and a heat resistant coating on the chestpiece. You can also make out a fant name scratched onto the neckpiece normally where unit designations go. "Ranger Rick"

A duster often worn by the rangers. It�s main use is to protect the wearer against the sun and temperatures of the Mojave Desert, but it�s thick enough to provide additional protection in a firefight as well. This one looks a bit rougher than most, having seen a lot of action. The colors are also different from those of the NCR Veterans, it has a darker brown color. It also has the badge of the Desert Rangers still pinned on the chest. A relic of a bygone era. On the inside of the duster someone has hurridly written "Rick".

An enclosed military helmet with built-in low light optics and a gas mask worn by NCR Rangers. It offers better protection in comparison to the Trooper helmet but is significantly rarer. This version seems to have the enhanced low vis optics added in as well as a paint job. Theres a name written on the standard NCR item tag "Rick" as well as the words "FORGIVE ME MASHED" painted on the front of the helmet. Makes you wonder what happend to this Mashed person...

A set of clothes made from tough materials such as leathers. Seems as though this one is stronger and tougher than standard uniforms. The standard pants have also been replaced by a pair of worn blue jeans and the shirt has been dyed. The name Rick has also been scribbled on the standard NCR item tag on the inside of the shirt.
Paper money made and distributed by the New California Republic government as a means to replace caps. While not worth as much as the cap it is still a means of currency and should be valued as such.

The anti-materiel rifle is a high-calibre bolt-action precision rifle, originally intended for the use against military equipment. Naturally, it can be, and is, used against living targets. There are relatively few anti-materiel rifles in circulation in the wasteland, most of them in the hands of elite forces of larger factions or high-end mercenaries. As a pure sniper rifle it is very unwieldy and cannot be used effectively in close combat. Over long distances, however, its accuracy and potency are unparalleled, making it a truly fearsome weapon when used by an experienced shooter.

A powerful bolt-action rifle with an attached iron sight intended for hunting big game. It is durable, accurate and easy to use and maintain weapon and thus a popular choice not only for hunters, but for many snipers in the NCR, Legion, and other groups. This particular design is boasted by the NCR recon snipers, featuring the two headed bear insignia.

A rare, large caliber double-action revolver gifted to NCR rangers after 20 years of service. Itâ€™s a beautifully made weapon and decorated with NCR symbols and quotes. However, itâ€™s by no means just for show. In fact, it is one of the most powerful handguns in the whole wasteland and a testament for the skills of the Republicâ€™s gun smiths.
A general-purpose gas-operated assault rifle for field or urban combat. It has good range, decent power, and good accuracy.
A crate of food from the New California Republic, standard issue.
A crate of weapons from the New California Republic, standard issue.
A crate of uniforms from the New California Republic, standard issue.
A crate of weapons from the New California Republic, standard issue.
A crate of uniforms from the New California Republic, standard issue.
A crate of uniforms from the New California Republic, standard issue.
A crate of uniforms from the New California Republic, standard issue.

Circa 2277, the New California Republic, in a decisive attack against Caesar's Legion during the First Battle of Hoover Dam, lured the bulk of the enemy into Boulder City, most of which was filled with high explosives, and detonated them, destroying the Legion's assault at the cost of many troopers who could not escape in time. Following the battle, the New California Republic built the memorial to honor the 107 Rangers and service members who lost their lives that day.

Vertibird is a designation for a series of vertical take-off and landing (VTOL) aircraft. These multipurpose tiltrotor aircraft were developed by the United States military and rapidly became a primary gunship and transport aircraft. This one is now in use by the New California Republic.
A standard Nuka-Cola in its iconic bottle. Provides refreshment and a mild stimulant effect from the unique blend of ingredients.
Nuka-Cola in a convenient aluminum can. Same great taste and energy boost as the bottled version, but easier to stack and transport.
A cherry-flavored variant of Nuka-Cola. The added fruit flavoring provides slight health benefits along with the signature energy boost. Particularly popular before the war.

The standard Nuka-Cola that took the world by storm before the war. This sweet, carbonated beverage provides a quick energy boost and a small amount of joy. The distinctive rocket-shaped bottle is instantly recognizable.
A tart cranberry-flavored Nuka-Cola variant. The berry tang balances the sweetness, giving a pleasant, slightly acidic bite favored in holiday marketing campaigns.
A rum-flavored alcoholic Nuka-Cola variant. Combines the stimulant effects of Nuka-Cola with alcohol, resulting in unique but potentially risky effects. Popular with adventurous drinkers.
A grape-flavored Nuka-Cola variant with a distinctive purple hue. The grape flavoring adds a sweet taste while maintaining the energizing properties of standard Nuka-Cola.

A chilled variant of Nuka-Cola. Cold, crisp, and refreshing â€” a pre-war marketing hit. Said to be made by a special cold-fermentation process that gives it a sharper fizz and a brisk, invigorating kick.
An orange-flavored Nuka-Cola with hints of citrus. The vitamin content from orange flavoring provides minor immune system benefits alongside the energizing effects.

The rare and potent Nuka-Cola Quantum, featuring a blue glow from mild radioactive isotopes. Provides a massive energy boost but with potential health risks. Highly sought after for its extreme effects.
A rare desert-region variant with a distinctive clear appearance and enhanced formula. Provides superior refreshment and energy in harsh conditions.
A stronger rum variant of Nuka-Cola with higher alcohol content. Less carbonation, more kick. Provides significant intoxication with mild stimulant effects.
A citrus-twisted variant of Nuka-Cola with bright lemon-lime notes. Popular for its zesty tang and slightly higher perceived refreshment.
A premium pre-war variant celebrating military victories. Enhanced with additional stimulants and a superior taste. Commands a high price for its exceptional effects.
A wild berry flavored Nuka-Cola with an aggressive marketing campaign. The exotic fruit blend provides enhanced taste and a solid energy boost.
A specialized cold fermentation cabinet used by Nuka-Cola bottling teams to create the Ice Cold variant. Works like a fermenting barrel but maintains cooler temperatures for a crisper product.

A powerful homebrewed alcoholic concoction made with Nuka-Cola as a base. Extremely potent and unpredictable, it can cause memory loss and blackouts. Only the desperate or foolhardy consume this regularly.

A simple red cap bearing the golden star emblem of the People's Republic of China. This practical headwear is standard issue for soldiers and low-ranking military personnel of the People's Liberation Army. The bright red fabric and prominent star design display allegiance to the state while providing basic protection from sun and light rain.

A practical crimson and gold jumpsuit worn by soldiers and low-ranking military personnel of the People's Liberation Army. The bright red fabric bears the iconic yellow star, symbolizing loyalty to the state. While offering minimal protection, it's designed for comfort during long duty shifts and provides basic insulation against the elements.

An officer's variant of the standard Chinese army jumpsuit, featuring additional decorative elements, shoulder boards, and reinforced fabric. Worn by military officers and high-ranking officials of the People's Liberation Army, this uniform commands respect and authority while maintaining the practical design of the standard issue. The added ornamentation and medals boost the wearer's confidence and negotiating presence.

A heavily insulated variant of the standard Chinese army jumpsuit, designed for operations in harsh winter conditions. This padded crimson uniform features a quilted construction with synthetic insulation, fur-lined collar, and reinforced seams. Originally developed for Chinese troops stationed in the frozen northern territories, it provides excellent protection against extreme cold while maintaining the distinctive red and gold color scheme of the People's Republic.

A naval service cap worn by submariners and sailors of the People's Liberation Army Navy. This water-resistant cap features the red star emblem and provides protection against ocean spray and harsh weather conditions. The dark navy blue color with red accents is instantly recognizable as belonging to China's naval forces.

A peaked service cap bearing the golden star emblem of the People's Republic of China. This formal headwear is worn by officers and high-ranking officials of the Chinese military. The rigid design and prominent insignia command respect and denote authority, making it a symbol of leadership within the ranks.

An advanced tactical helmet integrated with Chinese stealth technology. This sleek helmet features a reinforced visor, built-in rebreather, and sensor array. The hermetically sealed design provides protection against environmental hazards and can interface with stealth field systems when worn with compatible armor.

A cutting-edge piece of Chinese military technology, this form-fitting stealth suit incorporates advanced light-bending technology and sound-dampening materials. Developed by the People's Liberation Army for covert operations, the suit features a flexible polymer construction with integrated circuitry that provides active camouflage capabilities. Its lightweight design and mobility enhancements make it ideal for infiltration, reconnaissance, and assassination missions. The suit's signature crimson and black color scheme can shift to blend with surroundings when activated.

A specialized uniform worn by submariners of the People's Liberation Army Navy. This practical ensemble features water-resistant fabrics, reinforced stitching, and multiple sealed pockets designed to protect essential equipment in damp, confined submarine environments. The dark navy and crimson design includes reflective strips for emergency visibility and a high collar for protection against cold ocean winds. While not a full diving suit, it provides basic environmental protection and is valued by sailors, dock workers, and naval personnel.

A fur-lined winter cap featuring the iconic red star of the People's Republic of China. This warm ushanka is standard issue for Chinese military personnel operating in cold climates. The ear flaps provide excellent protection against frostbite, while the red exterior proudly displays allegiance to the state.

Pre-war Chinese currency, issued by the People's Republic of China. These crisp red banknotes feature the portrait of Chairman Mao and various symbols of communist pride. Despite the passage of centuries since the Great War, yuan notes remain a recognized medium of exchange in regions influenced by Chinese culture and among traders who deal with remnants of the former People's Republic.

A hardy desert plant found throughout the Mojave, banana yucca produces sweet, starchy fruit that is a staple for survival. Its thick leaves and resilience make it a reliable source of nutrition for wasteland wanderers.

This round, spiny cactus stores water in its thick stem and can be found scattered across arid landscapes. Barrel cactus fruit is edible and provides a modest but vital source of hydration and nutrition in the harsh wasteland.
A rare desert flower prized for its medicinal properties. Broc flower is a key ingredient in healing powders and is sought after by wasteland survivors for its restorative effects.
A tough, wild gourd found in arid regions. While not especially tasty, its seeds and flesh can be eaten in a pinch, and it is sometimes used in traditional recipes by wastelanders.
A nutritious root vegetable cultivated by survivors for its sweet flavor and versatility. Carrots are a staple crop in many wasteland settlements.
The delicate flower of the carrot plant, sometimes found growing wild. Its seeds are used for propagation and can be a rare treat for foragers.
A tart, red berry cultivated in wasteland farms for its nutritional value and use in preserves. Cranberries are a resilient crop, thriving in moist soil.
A mutated fungus that emits a faint, eerie glow. Glowing mushrooms are found in irradiated caves and are sometimes used in wasteland remedies, though they may be mildly radioactive.
A versatile vegetable grown for its edible flesh and use as a container. Gourds are a common sight in wasteland gardens and provide a reliable food source.
A hardy desert shrub with sweet pods. Honey mesquite is used by wastelanders for food, fuel, and even medicine, making it a valuable resource in arid regions.
A spicy pepper grown in wasteland gardens, jalapenos add heat and flavor to many survivor recipes. Hardy and productive, they are a favorite among cultivators.
A staple crop in the wasteland, maize is grown for its hearty ears and versatility. It is a key ingredient in many survivor meals and can be stored for long periods.
A sweet, juicy fruit grown in wasteland gardens. Melons are prized for their refreshing taste and high water content, making them a valuable crop in arid regions.
A strange, mutated fern found in irradiated areas. Its fronds are tough and sometimes used in makeshift remedies, though caution is advised due to possible contamination.
A genetically modified fruit, mutfruit is a staple crop in the Fallout universe. Its sweet, tangy flavor and high yield make it a favorite among wasteland farmers.
A resilient desert succulent, Nevada agave is harvested for its sweet sap and fibrous leaves. Wastelanders use it for food, drink, and crafting.
A spiny cactus found in the Mojave, prized for its edible pads and sweet fruit. Prickly pear is a vital source of hydration and nutrition for desert dwellers.
A hardy, mutated grain crop grown for its tough stalks and nutritious seeds. Razorgrain is a staple in many wasteland settlements, used for bread and porridge.
A protein-rich bean crop, siltbeans are grown in the wasteland for their nutritional value and ability to thrive in poor soil. They are a key ingredient in many survivor meals.
A mutated berry found in marshy areas, tarberries are prized for their sweet flavor and mild radioactivity. Used in unique wasteland recipes and sometimes for trade.
A hybrid of tomato and potato, the tato is a staple crop in the Fallout universe. Its hearty tubers and tangy fruit are a reliable food source for survivors.
A gnarled desert root with potent medicinal properties. Xander root is a key ingredient in healing powders and is highly valued by wasteland healers.
Medicinal flower harvested from broc flower plants. Used in healing powders.
Seeds and flesh harvested from buffalo gourd plants. Edible in a pinch.
A sweet, nutritious root vegetable harvested from carrot plants. A staple food in the wasteland.
Delicate flower harvested from wild carrot plants. Used for propagation and as a rare treat.
A tart, red berry harvested from cranberry plants. Used in preserves and survivor meals.
Sweet, starchy fruit harvested from banana yucca plants. A staple for survival.
Edible fruit harvested from barrel cactus. Provides hydration and nutrition.
Mutated fungus harvested from glowing mushroom plants. Emits a faint glow and may be mildly radioactive.
A versatile vegetable harvested from gourd plants. Edible and useful as a container.
Sweet pods harvested from honey mesquite shrubs. Used for food, fuel, and medicine.
A spicy pepper harvested from jalapeno plants. Adds heat to wasteland recipes.
Hearty ears of maize harvested from maize plants. A key ingredient in survivor meals.
A sweet, juicy fruit harvested from melon plants. Prized for its refreshing taste.
Tough fronds harvested from mutated fern plants. Sometimes used in makeshift remedies.
A genetically modified fruit harvested from mutfruit trees. Sweet and tangy.
Sweet sap and fibrous leaves harvested from Nevada agave. Used for food, drink, and crafting.
Edible pads and sweet fruit harvested from prickly pear cactus. Vital for desert dwellers.
Tough stalks and nutritious seeds harvested from razorgrain plants. Used for bread and porridge.
Protein-rich beans harvested from siltbean plants. Thrives in poor soil.
Mutated berry harvested from tarberry plants. Sweet and mildly radioactive.
A hybrid tuber harvested from tato plants. Hearty and tangy.
Gnarled root harvested from xander root plants. Highly valued for its medicinal properties.

A reinforced exoskeletal frame designed to support the weight and systems of advanced power armor. On its own, the Power Armor Frame offers basic protection and increased carrying capacity, but it truly shines when fitted with full armor plating. Essential for anyone hoping to operate pre-war power armor suits in the wasteland.

An early model of power armor helmet, the T45 offers solid protection against most threats. Its bulky design is a testament to pre-war engineering, providing reliable defense at the cost of some comfort and mobility.

The T-45 was the first power armor model deployed in the field, offering soldiers unprecedented protection and strength. Though now considered outdated, its heavy plating and servo-assisted joints still provide formidable defense and enhanced mobility to any wastelander lucky enough to find a working suit.
The T51 helmet is a refined evolution of power armor technology, offering superior protection and improved ergonomics over earlier models. Favored for its balance of defense and comfort.

The T-51 is a legendary pre-war power armor, renowned for its superior protection and advanced design. Its titanium composite shell and improved servos make it lighter and more durable than earlier models, granting its wearer unmatched resilience and strength on the battlefield.
The T60 helmet represents advanced power armor development, featuring enhanced armor plating and improved environmental sealing. Designed for frontline combat in hazardous conditions.

The T-60 represents the pinnacle of pre-war military engineering, combining the best features of earlier models with enhanced armor and reliability. Favored by the Brotherhood of Steel, the T-60 offers exceptional protection, advanced environmental sealing, and a menacing presence on any battlefield.
The X01 helmet is the pinnacle of power armor headgear, boasting cutting-edge materials and maximum protection. Its intimidating design and advanced systems make it a rare and valuable asset.
T-51 power armor with a paint scheme inspired by the iconic Abraxo Cleaner branding. Clean, crisp, and unmistakable - this corporate paint job brings pre-war advertising aesthetics to the wasteland.
T-51 Power Paints Abraxo Power Armor
A T-51 power armor helmet with Abraxo Cleaner branding. Clean, crisp, and unmistakable corporate aesthetics.
T-51 power armor emblazoned with dazzling flame decals that turn heads and strike fear into the hearts of enemies. This iconic hot rod design brings wasteland style to the battlefield.

The X-01 is the most advanced power armor ever developed before the Great War, shrouded in secrecy and rarely seen outside top-secret military installations. Its cutting-edge materials and systems provide unmatched protection, strength, and intimidation, making it the ultimate prize for any wasteland survivor.
A specialized station for storing and displaying power armor sets. Allows quick swapping of armor pieces between storage and use.

A compact nuclear battery used to power pre-war technology, most notably power armor. These cylindrical power cells contain a highly efficient fusion reaction that can provide energy for extended periods. Extremely valuable and sought after by those who maintain pre-war equipment. Cannot be recharged with current technology.

A sealed pre-packaged meal consisting of ground beef steak with gravy, another staple of American cuisine. Itâ€™s very filling and quite tasty, making it a popular dish even centuries after the war. The BIG BOY meal comes with a side of InstaMash!

A surprisingly well-preserved fast-food product from before the war. Itâ€™s a rectangular box filled with a mix of macaroni and cheese, a staple of pre-war American cuisine and as such is a common sight in the wasteland. This seems to be the Extra Cheezy variation, adding slightly more calories to your meal.

A surprisingly well-preserved fast-food product from before the war. Itâ€™s a rectangular box filled with a mix of macaroni and cheese, a staple of pre-war American cuisine and as such is a common sight in the wasteland.

A tin can full of processed meat. Its main ingredients are pork and ham pressed into form and hold together by gelatine. Itâ€™s filled with enough preservatives to be edible over 200 years after its fabrication and is a common and popular ration for soldiers in the NCR due to its high calorie content.
A high-quality packaged meal, manufactured for use in survival situations. While it never rots, it can still deteriorate if left outside. Great for traveling.

Candied apples contained in a red cardboard box, a popular pre-war snack, made by the Dandy Boy company. The staggering amount of sugar and preservatives added to the product ensures its edibility even 200 years later.

Candied apples contained in a red cardboard box, a popular pre-war snack, made by the Dandy Boy company. The staggering amount of sugar and preservatives added to the product ensures its edibility even 200 years later. This variation seems to have a delicious caramel shell!
Pre-war snack cakes packed up in a small box. Bite-sized pieces of cake with icing, these snacks are nearly unperishable and still serve as a sugary, sweet treat 200 years after their making.

Pre-war snack cakes packed up in a small box. Bite-sized pieces of cake with icing, these snacks are nearly unperishable and still serve as a sugary, sweet treat 200 years after their making. Be sure to look out for this version as it is the limited edition Buzzbite Flavor!
Fancy Lad Snack Cakes Buzzbite Flavor

A small green package with logo symbolizing a radioactive hazard sign. However, its contents are relatively harmless, apart from the omnipresent background radiation. The small gum drops contained within are sweet and tasty and have endured the ravages of time quite well. A sugary snack for wasteland explorers.

A small green package with logo symbolizing a radioactive hazard sign. However, its contents are relatively harmless, apart from the omnipresent background radiation. The small gum drops contained within are sweet and tasty and have endured the ravages of time quite well. A sugary snack for wasteland explorers. This popular pre-war flavor features the mountain battlefields of Anchorage Alaska.

A box of freeze-dried powdered mashed potatoes. This convenience food product proved to be edible two centuries after the war and can be found in many locations in the wasteland. Best paired with Salisbury Steak!

A box of freeze-dried powdered mashed potatoes with bacon added for extra flavor and texture! This convenience food product proved to be edible two centuries after the war and can be found in many locations in the wasteland. Best paired with Salisbury Steak!

A can of beans stewed in a tomato sauce with chunks of bacon and salt-cured pork. A product from the Greasy Prospector company, Pork nâ€™Beans were sold in a tin can with a blue and red label and remain edible to this day.

A can of beans stewed in a tomato sauce with chunks of bacon and salt-cured pork. A product from the Greasy Prospector company, Pork nâ€™Beans were sold in a tin can with a blue and red label and remain edible to this day. Based on the packaging this seems to be the "Hot N Spicy Flavor".
Pork N Beans Hot N Spicy Flavor

A box of Spring Valley potato chips. An iconic pre-war snack composed of thin potato slices salted and baked in oil. Due to their immense popularity before the war theyâ€™re still a common sight in the wasteland.

A tube of Spring Valley potato chips in lightly salted falvor. An iconic pre-war snack composed of thin potato slices salted and baked in oil. Due to their immense popularity before the war theyâ€™re still a common sight in the wasteland.
A high-quality packaged meal, manufactured for use in survival situations. While it never rots, it can still deteriorate if left outside. Great for traveling.

A red, white and blue box of pre-war breakfast cereals, famous (and notorious) for their high sugar content. While such an amount of sugar surely is unhealthy, it also ensured that theyâ€™re edible to this day. Due to its status as the dominant breakfast cereal, it can still be found nearly everywhere in the wasteland.

A red and blue package with dried whole deviled eggs made by YumYum. Despite being hard-boiled eggs filled with a paste made from egg yolks, mustard and mayonnaise, theyâ€™re still edible for reasons best left to discretion.

A yellow and gold package with dried whole deviled eggs made by YumYum with a hint of sour cream added flavor. Despite being hard-boiled eggs filled with a paste made from egg yolks, mustard and mayonnaise, theyâ€™re still edible for reasons best left to discretion.
Yum Yum Deviled Eggs Sour Cream Flavor
A gas mask fitted with a reinforced metal skull plate. The combination of functional protection and terrifying aesthetics makes this a favored piece of headgear among raider elites.

A basic leather helmet, with a young bighorner's skull affixed to the top for decoration. The skull has a red bullseye on it, most often worn by the fiends, a particularly vicious group of raiders originating from the Mojave wasteland. It doesnâ€™t protect well, but looks quite intimidating, which is more important to most raiders anyway.

A basic leather helmet, with a young bighorner's skull affixed to the top for decoration. The skull has a red bullseye on it, most often worn by the fiends, a particularly vicious group of raiders originating from the Mojave wasteland. It doesnâ€™t protect well, but looks quite intimidating, which is more important to most raiders anyway.

A basic leather helmet, with a young bighorner's skull affixed to the top for decoration. The skull has a red bullseye on it, most often worn by the fiends, a particularly vicious group of raiders originating from the Mojave wasteland. It doesnâ€™t protect well, but looks quite intimidating, which is more important to most raiders anyway.
A makeshift power armor helmet assembled from salvaged parts. While not as protective as military-grade helmets, it's still far superior to conventional raider headgear.
An unusual power armor helmet that has been modified with a Mr. Fuzzy mascot theme. Despite its absurd appearance, it provides the same protection as standard raider power armor helmets.

A repurposed welding mask. It offers fairly good protection from the front but leaves the back of the head virtually unprotected. It encourages its wearer to storm blindly at the enemy, as retreating becomes a bad, and often fatal, idea.

Low-tech, homemade armour with a barbaric appearance. It varies in quality and appearance with most consisting of scrap metal plates melded into armor pieces reinforced by metal bars along with wires fastened together by leather straps.The heavy raider armor is usually reserved for leaders and others who bellow orders to the gang. While it protects a whole lot better than the other version, it is also bulky and heavy, severely impairing the wearers freedom of movement.

Low-tech, homemade armour with a barbaric appearance. It varies in quality and appearance with most consisting of scrap metal plates melded into armor pieces reinforced by metal bars along with wires fastened together by leather straps. The light version of the raider armor provides only basic protection but is less heavy than other variants. Not the best armor, but certainly better than nothing.

Low-tech, homemade armour with a barbaric appearance. It varies in quality and appearance with most consisting of scrap metal plates melded into armor pieces reinforced by metal bars along with wires fastened together by leather straps.This version offers slightly more protection than the lighter version, but still isnâ€™t that great. Plus, it weighs a whole lot more
A piece of tattered clothing, wrapped up in some places with duct tape, a wide leather belt, and black combat boots. These clothes are often worn by raiders and provide barely any protection.

A leather coat covered in an excessive number of rags, chains, and metal plates with pieces of rebar welded onto them. As far as raider armors go, this one protects fairly well, but in turn is quite heavy. Usually worn by raiders with (some) self-preservation instinct.
Comprised of a firefighter's helmet worn on top of a gas mask, the blastmaster helmet does provide decent protection and is often covered with fire-retardant substances.

A long leather duster worn by raider leaders and those who have survived long enough to earn some respect. Offers better protection than basic raider armor while maintaining an intimidating appearance.

A crude leather outfit cobbled together from various animal hides and scraps. While offering minimal protection, it's lightweight and allows for quick movement - essential for hit-and-run raider tactics.
A piece of tattered clothing, wrapped up in some places with duct tape, a wide leather belt, and black combat boots. These clothes are often worn by raiders and provide barely any protection.
A piece of tattered clothing, wrapped up in some places with duct tape, a wide leather belt, and black combat boots. These clothes are often worn by raiders and provide barely any protection.

A crude suit of power armor cobbled together from scavenged parts, rusted plating, and salvaged T-45 components. While it offers decent protection for a wastelander, it lacks the refinement and reliability of proper military power armor. The makeshift construction leaves gaps in coverage, and the improvised repairs make it noticeably heavier and more cumbersome than its pre-war counterparts. Still, in the wasteland, any power armor is better than none.
A power armor helmet that has been crudely modified by raiders. The internal systems barely function, but it still provides excellent protection against most threats.
A leather skull cap with goggles and handlebars jutting out of the sides of the head. It is not very protective, and its main use is intimidation.

This archaic armor consists of metal chaps and a leather plate of sorts worn on the left shoulder. It leaves the shoulders of its wearer bare and unprotected. Most raiders care little about that though, as its primary use is intimidation of the foe.

Makeshift armor constructed from scavenged tire rubber and metal scraps. The thick rubber provides decent protection against blunt impacts while remaining surprisingly flexible. Popular among raiders who value mobility over heavy protection.

A helmet constructed of several pieces of heavy burlap crudely stitched together. It also includes eye protection that looks like it was torn from a gas mask and tufts of hair. Not very potent armor, but it does intimidate the foe quite well.
A weathered gas mask with a corroded skull plate attached. Despite its deteriorated condition, it still provides adequate protection and maintains the intimidating raider aesthetic.
A simple hood often worn by raiders. It provides barely any protection against bullets but is (barely) better than no helmet at all.

A more refined raider power armor variant favored by the Scabbers gang. Built from better-maintained salvage and reinforced with additional scrap plating, it offers improved protection while maintaining the characteristic ramshackle appearance. The Scabbers take pride in their armor's intimidating spikes and rust-stained panels, wearing them as badges of honor. Despite being heavier than standard raider power armor, it provides noticeably better protection against the dangers of the wasteland.
A heavily corroded power armor helmet covered in rust and decay. Despite its deteriorated appearance, the core structure remains solid and provides substantial protection.

The pinnacle of raider power armor engineering, the Waster variant combines salvaged military-grade components with surprisingly competent craftsmanship. Though still crude by pre-war standards, it approaches the protection level of T-45 power armor while maintaining the brutal aesthetic raiders prefer. The heavy construction and layered plating make it a formidable sight on the battlefield. Waster bosses and elite raiders prize these suits above all other armor.
A battle-worn power armor helmet showing signs of extensive use and makeshift repairs. While not pristine, it remains a formidable piece of protective gear.

Brahmin serve a vital environmental niche in the wasteland, they form the foundation of survival for many species. Forming the backbone of the New Californian economy; brahmin can carry large loads for a far greater distance than other creatures. Brahmin droppings are great fertilizer and fuel for campfires; the fumes from their feces are also used to synthesize jet; while their hide and hair can be used to make tents, leather clothing, saddle bags, or a bizarre brahmin-looking disguise.

A massive, heavily reinforced cage designed to contain the largest and most dangerous wasteland predators. Originally designed for pre-war zoos and research facilities, these cages feature reinforced steel construction and advanced containment systems. Only the most skilled engineers can properly construct these formidable enclosures.

A reinforced cage capable of holding medium-sized wasteland creatures. Built with thicker steel bars and improved locking mechanisms, this cage can safely contain animals that would easily escape from smaller enclosures. Popular among wasteland traders who deal in exotic mutated pets and working animals.

A sturdy metal cage designed to safely capture and contain small wasteland creatures. Originally used by pre-war researchers and hunters, these cages have proven invaluable for wastelanders looking to domesticate the various mutated animals roaming the post-nuclear landscape. Requires regular feeding to maintain captured animals.
A distinctive hat worn by the mysterious stranger, providing a subtle boost to accuracy.

An advanced iteration of the Pip-Boy 3000, the Mark IV features enhanced processing power and a more refined interface. Its improved data management systems provide better work coordination and faster information processing. The Mark IV was considered top-of-the-line before the war. This model offers superior performance compared to earlier versions, with enhanced computational capabilities.

The Pip-Boy 3000 model was the most common personal information processor issued to Vault dwellers before the war. This rugged wrist-mounted computer features a monochrome green display and provides essential data management capabilities. Its simple but reliable interface assists with work coordination and task management. This is the classic model known for its durability and ease of use.
A powerful 14mm round for high-caliber pistols.
A massive .50 Action Express round for the most powerful handguns.
An old Nuka Cola bottle cap. What used to be garbage is now the most commonly used currency in the wastes today.
A Vault-Tec collectors trading card. This is just a common one, but maybe it'll be worth something to someone.
A Vault-Tec collectors trading card and this is a legendary one! This is a one of a kind piece, im sure its worth more than a few caps!
A Vault-Tec collectors trading card, this is a Rare one. Not the most valuable, but still an amazing find!
A Vault-Tec collectors edition lunchbox. It's a little dirty, but still opens and may have something useful inside!
A Vault-Tec collectors edition lunchbox. It's in Mint Condition, there's probably something valuable inside!
Mint Condition Vault-Tec Collectors Edition Lunchbox
A virtual reality pod designed for power armor training simulations, enhancing the user's combat skills and proficiency with power armor.
An old, destroyed military truck. Used by the great nations before the bombs droped.
An old, destroyed military Vertibid. Used by the great nations before the bombs droped.
A workbench for making complex weapons.
A cooking station fueled with wood.
A cooking station fueled with wood.
A workbench equipped with advanced tools for producing technological marvels from simpler materials.
A workbench for tailoring clothes by hand. Works at 50% of the speed of an electric tailoring bench.

Vertibird is a designation for a series of vertical take-off and landing (VTOL) aircraft. These multipurpose tiltrotor aircraft were developed by the United States military and rapidly became a primary gunship and transport aircraft.
A heavy reinforced concrete wall designed for military bunkers. Extremely durable and provides excellent protection. These walls were commonly used in pre-war military installations.

An advanced unarmed weapon that combines pneumatic power with an explosive shotgun shell. When the fist connects, it fires a shell directly into the target, causing massive trauma. The ultimate close-quarters devastator.
A pair of padded boxing gloves, originally designed for sport. While they won't cause lethal damage easily, they're excellent for non-lethal takedowns and can still break a jaw with a solid punch.

An experimental energy weapon that wraps around the hand. It generates a field that disrupts matter on contact, causing severe tissue damage and internal injuries. The distinctive blue glow betrays its advanced technology.

A heavy-duty industrial prosthetic fist designed for construction and demolition work. While not originally intended as a weapon, its hydraulic actuators and reinforced metal construction make it devastatingly effective in combat, crushing anything it strikes.

A pre-war pneumatic gauntlet that uses pressurized gas to deliver devastating punches. When the fist impacts, it releases a burst of compressed air that multiplies the force several times over, capable of shattering bones and denting armor with each blow.

A leather glove modified with electrical components that deliver a powerful shock on contact. Originally developed for non-lethal crowd control, it can stun and incapacitate targets. The crackling blue energy is both intimidating and effective.

A distinguished uniform issued to generals of the United States Army, symbolizing command and tradition. Its immaculate condition and ornate details reflect the prestige and authority of America's military leadership.

A winterized uniform issued to generals of the United States Army, symbolizing command and tradition. Enhanced with insulated lining and weatherproof materials, it provides warmth and protection in cold climates while maintaining the prestige and authority of America's military leadership.

The standard-issue US Army Combat Armor of 2077, featuring advanced composite plating and modular design. Widely deployed across conflict zones, it offers robust protection against ballistic and energy threats while maintaining mobility. Integrated with environmental seals and tactical interfaces, this armor represents the pinnacle of military technology in the United States.

The standard-issue US Army Combat Helmet of 2077, designed to complement the advanced composite plating and modular features of the armor. Widely deployed alongside its armor counterpart, it provides robust protection against ballistic and energy threats while maintaining comfort and tactical functionality. Integrated with environmental seals and communication interfaces, this helmet exemplifies the pinnacle of military headgear technology in the United States.

A distinguished cap issued to generals of the United States Army, symbolizing command and tradition. Its immaculate condition and ornate details reflect the prestige and authority of America's military leadership.

A United States Army variant of the legendary T-51 power armor. Painted in military olive drab and bearing the insignia of the US Armed Forces, this suit represents the standard issue for Army mechanized infantry units. The T-51's titanium composite shell and advanced servos provided unmatched battlefield superiority during the Resource Wars, making its wearers nearly unstoppable forces of pre-war military might.

A United States Army variant of the T-51 power armor helmet. Painted in military olive drab with Army insignia, this helmet provides the legendary protection of the T-51 system while marking its wearer as a member of the pre-war American mechanized infantry.
US Army T-51 Power Armor Helmet

The ultimate expression of United States Army power armor technology before the Great War. This T-60 variant bears the proud colors and markings of the US military, issued only to the most elite mechanized infantry divisions. Combining superior protection with advanced environmental systems and intimidating presence, the T-60 represented the cutting edge of American military engineering and the backbone of armored operations during the final days of the old world.

The helmet component of the United States Army's T-60 power armor. Bearing the colors and insignia of the pre-war military, this advanced helmet combines cutting-edge heads-up display technology with maximum protection, representing the peak of American power armor engineering before the Great War.
US Army T-60 Power Armor Helmet
Heavy armor worn by United States Marine Corps personnel. Provides excellent protection against ballistic threats.

The standard-issue US Marine Corps Combat Helmet of 2077, engineered specifically for the elite Marine Corps with enhanced ballistic protection and amphibious operation capabilities. Features advanced composite materials, integrated communication systems, environmental seals, and a built-in atmospheric filtration system that provides protection against chemical agents, toxic gases, and environmental hazards. This helmet represents the pinnacle of Marine Corps tactical headgear technology.

A specialized diving helmet used by United States Marine Corps combat divers and underwater demolition teams. Features an integrated breathing system, reinforced visor, and communication equipment designed for underwater operations. Provides excellent protection against environmental hazards while maintaining clear vision and communication capabilities during amphibious assaults.

A lighter variant of the US Marine Corps armor, sacrificing some protection for improved mobility and reduced weight. Designed for rapid deployment scenarios and extended field operations where agility is paramount. Still provides substantial ballistic protection while allowing marines to operate with greater speed and endurance.

A specialized wetsuit worn by United States Marine Corps divers and amphibious assault teams. Made from advanced neoprene composite, it provides thermal insulation and environmental protection while maintaining flexibility for underwater operations. Designed to be worn under marine armor during amphibious missions.

An elegant formal suit worn by the President of the United States. Made from the finest materials with exquisite tailoring, it represents the dignity and authority of the highest office in the nation. The suit is both comfortable and imposing, suitable for state functions and diplomatic meetings.

Advanced tactical armor issued to elite United States Secret Service agents tasked with protecting the highest levels of government. Engineered from composite alloys and reinforced plating, this armor provides exceptional ballistic and energy protection without sacrificing mobility. The low-profile design allows agents to blend into formal settings while remaining combat-ready at a moment's notice.

An advanced tactical helmet issued to United States Secret Service agents. Featuring reinforced composite construction, integrated communication systems, and ballistic face protection, this helmet provides comprehensive head protection while maintaining situational awareness. The sleek matte-black finish and professional appearance allow agents to remain imposing yet discreet during protective operations.

A specialized tactical underarmor worn by United States Secret Service agents beneath their armor plating. Constructed from advanced ballistic weave and moisture-wicking fibers, it provides a baseline layer of protection and climate control. The carefully designed cut ensures full range of motion while maintaining professional appearance under formal attire when needed.

The pinnacle of pre-war American power armor technology, the T-65 was developed exclusively for elite Secret Service operatives assigned to the most critical national security operations. Incorporating cutting-edge materials science and engineering far beyond standard military issue, this armor provides unmatched protection and strength enhancement. Its distinctive profile is instantly recognizable to those familiar with the inner workings of the United States government.

The helmet component of the legendary T-65 Secret Service Power Armor. This masterwork of engineering incorporates the most advanced heads-up display systems, threat detection arrays, and life support equipment ever fielded by the United States. The multi-layered composite construction provides superior protection against all known threats while maintaining perfect environmental sealing and combat awareness.

The winterized variant of the US Army Combat Armor of 2077, featuring advanced composite plating, modular design, and enhanced insulation for extreme cold. Outfitted with weatherproof materials and environmental seals, it offers robust protection against ballistic and energy threats while ensuring soldier mobility and warmth in harsh winter conditions.

The winterized variant of the US Army Combat Helmet, issued in 2077 for operations in extreme cold environments. Enhanced with advanced thermal insulation, weatherproofing, and an integrated facial protection system, it maintains the robust protection and sophisticated features of the standard model while ensuring comfort and operational effectiveness in harsh winter conditions. The built-in thermal faceguard offers superior coverage against wind and frostbite, while integrated environmental seals and communication interfaces remain standard, making this helmet essential for cold-weather deployments.
A white variant of the US Army General Cap, issued  for winter use. It retains the same distinguished appearance and authority as the standard cap, with no additional insulation or weatherproofing.
U.S. Dollar bill, standard currency in America. Used for everyday transactions and trade.

Constructed from hardened alloy steel and precision-machined components, this 10mm pistol combines reliability and enhanced stopping power with improved durability and armor penetration compared to field-grade sidearms. Favored by security forces.

Built from hardened alloy steel and precision-machined components, this assault rifle delivers consistent, high-velocity fire under harsh conditions. Balanced ergonomics and a tuned gas system keep recoil manageable, making rapid follow-up shots practical even in heavy combat. Favored by hardened units for its durability and ability to punch through field-grade armor.
A crate of food from the United States of America, standard issue.

A military Meal Ready to Eat from the United States Armed Forces. Individually packaged and designed for field use, it contains a complete meal with multiple components including an entree, sides, dessert, and accessories. More filling and palatable than standard survival packs, though it will never win any culinary awards. Never rots but can deteriorate if left outside.
A crate of armor from the United States Army, standard issue.
A crate of uniforms from the United States Army, standard issue.
A crate of weapons from the United States of America, standard issue.
A crate of armor from the United States Marine Corps, standard issue.
A crate of uniforms from the United States Marine Corps, standard issue.

Vertibird is a designation for a series of vertical take-off and landing (VTOL) aircraft. These multipurpose tiltrotor aircraft were developed by the United States military and rapidly became a primary gunship and transport aircraft.

This grand memorial commemorates the pivotal battle in Anchorage, where our brave US forces, led by General Constantine Chase, overcame the invading Chinese army commanded by General Jingwei. It stands as a tribute to the courage and sacrifice of those who defended our homeland and marks the turning point that secured our freedom.

A dark, rich sausage made from the blood and meat of various wasteland creatures. While nutritious and filling, the questionable ingredients and preparation methods can lead to minor toxic buildup. A favorite among raiders and desperate survivors.

A hearty pot pie filled with tender brahmin meat, tatos, and razorgrain. This comfort food from the wasteland provides a satisfying meal that reminds settlers of better times. The rich brahmin meat provides a temporary boost to work speed.

A paste made from various insects ground into a protein-rich but unappetizing mush. It's filling and surprisingly nutritious, though the taste leaves much to be desired. The questionable hygiene can cause minor toxic buildup.

A simple but comforting cup of noodles with chunks of rad chicken meat. Quick to prepare and easy on the stomach, this meal reminds people of simpler times. The warm broth provides a small mood boost.

A spicy, aggressive stew made with whatever meat is available, heavily seasoned with jalapeÃ±os and wasteland spices. Named after the infamous Fiend cook, this meal gives a temporary boost to melee damage and pain tolerance, but the toxic ingredients cause buildup.

Deep-fried rad chicken wings with a crispy golden coating. A popular snack in wasteland settlements, these wings are fried in vegetable oil until perfectly crispy. The indulgent meal provides a mood boost.

A premium meal of grilled deathclaw steak served with fresh eggs. Deathclaw meat is incredibly rare and prized for its flavor and nutritional value. This meal provides significant stat boosts including increased movement speed and melee damage for a time.
Tender deathclaw steak served alongside razorgrain. The combination of premium meat and hearty grain creates a satisfying and nutritious meal that boosts work efficiency and provides stamina.
A thin broth made from small wasteland creatures and scraps. While barely palatable and nearly flavorless, it prevents starvation. The questionable ingredients can cause minor toxic buildup.
Simple grilled meat skewered on a stick. Quick to prepare and easy to eat on the go, this basic meal is a staple of wasteland traders and travelers.
A basic cup of noodles made from razorgrain flour. While bland and unremarkable, it's cheap, filling, and quick to prepare. A wasteland staple.

A well-prepared steak from prime wasteland beast meat. Properly cooked and seasoned, this meal is a treat in the harsh wasteland. The high-quality protein provides a temporary boost to consciousness and blood pumping.

A lavish meal featuring premium steak served with grilled maize and roasted tatos. This well-balanced meal combines the best ingredients the wasteland has to offer. Provides significant stat boosts and mood improvement.
prime meat steak with corn and tatos
Simple grilled sausages made from ground wasteland meat and spices. A straightforward, filling meal that's easy to prepare and transport. Popular among caravans.
Part of the army uniform, this helmet offers a little protection.

A pre-war United States Army dress uniform in olive drab. The uniform consists of a military jacket with brass buttons and insignia, matching trousers, and a service cap. Despite its age, this formal military attire maintains its crisp appearance and represents the old world's military tradition.
An armored gasmask worn underneath a cloth hood. It provides decent protection against bullets and hazardous gases and similar substances and is most often worn by raiders.

A tattered and stitched baseball uniform with boots and knee pads, as they were common before the war. On its belt are pouches for storage. Itï¿½s not exactly a real armor and provides only a little protection but better than nothing.
Benny's suit is a black and white buffalo plaid suit jacket worn with light grey dress pants, white dress shirt, and black tie.

A basic outfit consisting of a (more or less) white shirt and patched overalls with Y-shaped suspenders complete with boots. The overalls are made of brahmin skin and are a common sight on ranches, farmsteads, and other areas where brahmins are held as livestock. Pretty basic clothing, cheap and widely available, but not very protective.
These are robes worn by Bright Follower ghouls. They are  slightly tattered and have a brownish-green color.
A light beige suit with a blue tie.
Rugged and sturdy clothing, often worn by caravaneers and other travellers. It does not provide a whole lot of protection but does not weigh down its wearer and is relatively common.
It appears as bondage harness on the chest with multiple leather bracelets and armbands. The bottom consists of a pair of low-rise, black leather pants.
A short-sleeved armor with pauldrons and chest plates covering the torso.

The combat armor was the standard-issue armor of the US army before the war. It is composed of armor plates made from defensive polymer attached to pieces of ballistic fibre. It offers more protection than metal armor, while being somewhat lighter. It is rare and expensive since few groups have the knowledge, and resources, to produce more and many suits are scavenged from pre-war military caches. Exotic and powerful, heavy combat armor offers superior protection against all kinds of damage. Obviously, it is very heavy, but only power armor guards its wearer better against the perils of the wasteland.
The combat armor helmet is the corresponding armor piece to the combat armor set. This model displays what is possibly a headlamp, attached to the left side above the brow line.

The combat armor was the standard-issue armor of the US army before the war. It is composed of armor plates made from defensive polymer attached to pieces of ballistic fibre. It offers more protection than metal armor, while being somewhat lighter. It is rare and expensive since few groups have the knowledge, and resources, to produce more and many suits are scavenged from pre-war military caches. While still rare, the light combat armor is the most available variant. It offers quite good protection while being lightweight.

The combat armor was the standard-issue armor of the US army before the war. It is composed of armor plates made from defensive polymer attached to pieces of ballistic fibre. It offers more protection than metal armor, while being somewhat lighter. It is rare and expensive since few groups have the knowledge, and resources, to produce more and many suits are scavenged from pre-war military caches. A matching helmet for the combat armor, it offers a bit of protection.
This outfit consists of black cowboy boots, black ripped tights worn under gray boxers, chains covering the wearer's chest which are connected to a spiked choker necklace, and a wrestling singlet.
A worn brown dress with slingback high-heels.
A tan blazer with brown lapels and slacks, complete with a dark brown bow tie.

Sturdy work clothes often worn by those settlers who make a living toiling in the fields. Lightweight and easy to make, this outfit is made to withstand the elements, not bullets (or claws and teeth).

Outfit invented by Rick, i don't know how to describe it, i'll ask him maybe
A gas mask to go with the gas ranger outfit.

A simple gas mask that covers the entire face, often seen worn by raiders. It is held on to the wearer by adjustable straps. It's not very protective against bullets and other physical attacks but provides medium protection against toxins and radiation.
A loincloth with a leather made bra.
Outfit worn by Jason Bright, it appears to resemble a weathered fancy gambler suit, without the tie.
A clean white lab coat with a blue undershirt and a brown tie. It provides a bonus to Intelligence while worn.
A lab coat with a dosimeter on the left breast and with a white undershirt and a black tie. It provides a bonus to Intelligence while worn
A lab coat worn by the followers of the apocalypse. It provides a bonus to science and medicine while worn.
Leather armor is a type of armor composed of various pieces of tanned animal hide stitched together. While providing less protection than metal armor, leather armor is lighter and easier to produce.

An ankle length coat, made from leather. It has a belt which is made from the seat belt of an airplane or a car. Itï¿½s mostly seen worn by settlers and other civilians. However, itï¿½s clothing, not armour and does not protect well against bullets.
An outfit stylized to resemble that of the Hubris Comics superhero Manta Man.

An outfit composed of a leather jacket with torn-off sleeves, a red sweater underneath a pair of brown pants and sturdy boots. Itï¿½s often worn by roving people and is designed to guarantee unimpaired freedom of movement, while still providing more protection than regular clothing.
A gray-blue suit top with a matching mini-skirt and ripped black fishnet tights.
A faded teal suit jacket and pants with a gray shirt underneath, as well as a strange collar that protects the neck.

A long leather jacket with the sleeves removed with a white t-shirt underneath, as well as some black pants and dark boots. Like other merc outfits, it provides less protection than full-on armor, but more than regular clothing and doesnï¿½t weigh down its wearer much.

Metal armor is composed of various metal plates attached to pieces of leather. While offering superior protection compared to leather armor, it is considerably heavier and more difficult to craft. Common materials include steel, iron, and scrap metal welded together.
The military fatigues consist of a dirty set of pea-green fatigues with black boots.
The outfit worn by the Mistress of Mystery, one of the longest-running and most popular superhero characters in the Hubris Comics universe.
A set of leopard print pajamas.
A set of leopard print pajamas.

This outfit consists of denim overalls, a brown satchel, and a long tan sleeve shirt underneath, as well as a pair of half boots. As its name implies, itï¿½s most often worn by the prospectors rummaging through the ruins of the old world. As such it is designed to provide protection against dust storms and other hazards, not against bullets or beasts.
This female outfit consists of a black leather crop-top with purple lacing down the sides of the sleeves and black leather lace-up short-shorts finished with a studded belt and spiked collar.
This male outfit consists of a sheriff's vest complete with the badge, jean short-shorts and cowboy boots.

A yellow full body suit complete with a helmet. While it does provide a modicum of protection against common dangers, such as bullets, its main use to shield its wearer against the invisible, and almost omnipresent, danger of radiation. It is quite rare and expensive as few groups in the wasteland possess the knowledge to create these suits.
A helmet designed to protect the wearer from radiation exposure. Part of the radiation suit set.
Overalls worn by the workers of the Red Rocket gas stations.
A more advanced version of the standard combat armor.

The helmet is a ballistic assembly, designed to protect the wearer from projectile, bladed weapons, and blunt trauma. An integrated polarized visor protects the user's eyes from the ordinary battlefield hazards of grit and dust.

Basic settler clothing worn by women in wasteland communities. Practical and durable, designed for everyday work while providing minimal protection from the elements.

An outfit consisting of a black undershirt and a pair of gray canvas pants underneath a long, brown leather overcoat with the sleeves rolled up above the elbow. It sports a sheriff badge on the right breast.

A pocketed leather coat with brown cargo pants and dirty wingtip shoes, with a satchel and metal wire covering the right arm. This outfit is often worn by scouts who roam the vast wasteland to find suitable locations for new settlements. While this is certainly a highly dangerous task, these clothes provide little protection.
A costume version of the outfit worn by The Inspector, a Hubris Comics superhero and member of The Unstoppables!

The wasteland doctor fatigues consist of a white t-shirt for males, usually bloodstained; a small brown satchel bag, and brown cargo pants with pouches attached to the belt, and boots. Its most often seen worn by doctors, or any people with some medical training, as real doctors a rarity in the wastes. These are work clothes and provide little protection.

It consists of a tan zipped-up hoodie and cargo pants tucked into riding boots. Due to its low cost and wide avail, it is very common to find, but provides little to no protection from any real danger.
A simple cap made of wrapped cloth, commonly worn by wastelanders for sun protection. Lightweight and provides basic protection from the elements.

A human twisted by prolonged exposure to toxic radiation. Their rotting flesh is a testament to their transformation, yet they persist - immune to toxins and healed by them. Some say they're already dead, just too stubborn to stop walking.

A ghoul whose mind has deteriorated beyond recognition. Driven by primal instincts, they are prone to sudden violent outbursts when their ferality overtakes them. Their rotting flesh heals through toxins, but their humanity is all but lost.
A rare and dangerous ghoul variant that emits constant radiation. Their bodies glow with an eerie green light as they damage nearby non-ghouls while healing other ghouls with their radioactive aura.

A feral glowing ghoul whose mind has deteriorated while their radioactive body continues to emit deadly radiation. Driven by primal instincts and prone to violent outbursts, they are among the most dangerous ghouls in the wasteland.
